document.document_summary: This issue of the Kuwait Today Official Gazette, dated April 20, 2025, provides a comprehensive overview of recent governmental and commercial activities in Kuwait. It includes new decree-laws, such as amendments to criminal procedures and an agreement with Austria on visa exemptions, alongside various ministerial and administrative decisions concerning internet services, capital markets, and public appointments. A significant portion is dedicated to official announcements and tenders from numerous ministries and public authorities, covering a wide array of projects, supplies, and services. Furthermore, the gazette details company liquidations, legal entity changes, commercial agency registrations, and minutes from general assembly meetings. It also features extensive sections on public auctions for vehicles and real estate, and judicial announcements, including criminal judgments and notices for lost documents.

chunks.title: Amending Certain Provisions of the Code of Criminal Procedures and Trials <orig>قانون الإجراءات والمحاكمات الجزائية</orig> Issued by Law No. 17 of 1960
chunks.text_en: - Having reviewed the Constitution, - And Amiri Order issued on 2 Dhu al-Qi'dah 1445 AH, corresponding to 10 May 2024 AD, - And the Penal Code issued by Law No. 16 of 1960, and its amending laws, - And the Code of Criminal Procedures and Trials issued by Law No. 17 of 1960, and its amending laws, - And the Social Insurance Law issued by Law No. 61 of 1976, and its amending laws, - And the Code of Civil and Commercial Procedures issued by Decree-Law No. 38 of 1980, and its amending laws, - And the Judicial Organization Law issued by Decree-Law No. 23 of 1990, and its amending laws, - And Law No. 19 of 2000 regarding the Support of National Labor and Encouraging its Employment in Non-Governmental Entities , amended by Law No. 32 of 2003, - And Law No. 2 of 2014 regarding Electronic Transactions , - And Decree No. 84 of 2024 regarding Ministerial Substitutions and Delegations , and its amending decrees, - And based on the submission of the Minister of Justice , - And after the approval of the Council of Ministers , - We have issued the following Decree-Law:

chunks.title: Article One
chunks.text_en: The texts of Articles (188 and 230) of the aforementioned Code of Criminal Procedures and Trials shall be replaced with the following two texts:

chunks.title: Article (188)
chunks.text_en: The period for objection is one week, commencing in misdemeanors from the date the absentee judgment is served on the convicted person. In felonies, it commences from the date of the convicted person's arrest if the judgment has not been served personally. If this period expires without the convicted person filing an objection, the judgment may only be appealed if it is subject to appeal. The absentee judgment shall be served personally on the convicted person, or via email, or by any other modern communication means capable of storage and retrieval, in accordance with the conditions and controls stipulated in the aforementioned Code of Civil and Commercial Procedures . If it is not possible to serve the judgment on the convicted person, the notification shall be delivered at their place of residence to any of their relatives or in-laws residing with them or to any of their followers. If none of them are found, or if those found refuse to receive the notification, the notification shall be published in the Official Gazette and affixed in prominent places in the area where it is located, and in a prominent place at their residence or workplace, and in any other place deemed appropriate for publication.

chunks.title: Article (230)
chunks.text_en: If the convicted person fails to pay the fine imposed, it shall be collected through compulsory execution on their assets. The Public Prosecution may collect it through monthly deductions not exceeding one-quarter of the amount the convicted person receives as salary, wage, labor support, or retirement pension. The President of the court that issued the judgment, or the Public Prosecutor or their authorized representative, may, upon the request of the convicted person, order the installment payment of the fine amount or its postponement for a reasonable period, provided that justification is presented, provided that the full amount of the fine is paid within five years.

chunks.title: Article Two
chunks.text_en: The Ministers – each within their jurisdiction – shall implement this Decree-Law, and it shall be published in the Official Gazette and shall come into force from the date of its publication. Amir of Kuwait Mishal Al-Ahmad Al-Jaber Al-Sabah Acting Prime Minister Fahad Yusuf Saud Al-Sabah Minister of Justice Counselor / Nasser Yousef Mohammed Al-Sumait <footer> Issued at Seif Palace on: 15 Shawwal 1446 AH Corresponding to: 13 April 2025 AD </footer># Explanatory Memorandum

chunks.title: For Decree-Law No. 62 of 2025 Amending Certain Provisions of the Code of Criminal Procedures and Trials Issued by Law No. 17 of 1960
chunks.text_en: for the year 1960. The amended Article (188) stipulates that the absentee judgment shall be served personally on the convicted person, or via email, or by any other modern communication means capable of storage and retrieval, in accordance with the rules set forth in the aforementioned Code of Civil and Commercial Procedures . The substituted Article (230) included a provision to address the problem revealed by practical experience regarding the accumulation of criminally imposed fines, which has reached an extreme level, as these amounts have become a heavy burden that is difficult to collect in many cases. What exacerbated the problem is that the failure to collect these fines from convicted persons has resulted in a set of serious legal issues, most notably the statute of limitations for criminal cases and the lapse of penalties due to the passage of time . These problems ultimately lead to the state losing its legitimate right to collect these amounts, even though these fines are a right enjoying a general privilege that the public treasury must collect for the benefit of the nation. For this reason, the substituted text came to specify more effective procedures for collecting criminal fines. It stipulated that if the convicted person fails to pay the imposed fine, it shall be collected through compulsory enforcement on their assets. It also permitted the Public Prosecution to collect it through monthly deductions not exceeding a quarter of the amount the convicted person receives as salary, wage, employment support , or retirement pension . Furthermore, this text granted the authority to the head of the court that issued the judgment, or to the Public Prosecutor or their authorized representative, to order the installment payment or deferral of the adjudicated amount for a reasonable period upon the request of the convicted person, provided they present justification for it, with the full adjudicated amount not to be delayed beyond five years. This ensures, on the one hand, that these amounts do not accumulate, and on the other hand, prevents the loss of state rights. The Code of Criminal Procedures and Trials is considered one of the fundamental pillars upon which criminal justice is based in any society. As such, it is not merely a collection of abstract legal texts, but rather the backbone that carries justice, establishes it in people's minds, and makes it effective in their lives. While law in general holds great importance in preserving rights and regulating relations between individuals, the Code of Criminal Procedures acquires special significance because it addresses the mechanism for applying these laws on the ground and formulates the method of applying justice with legal tools that ensure every individual can easily and readily access their rights. Perhaps one of the greatest roles played by the Code of Criminal Procedures and Trials is its emphasis on state sovereignty . The state cannot achieve true sovereignty unless it has the ability to enforce its laws, execute its judgments, and set the foundations of justice in motion to reach every rights-holder at all times and in all places. From this, it can be said that the Code of Criminal Procedures and Trials is the state's tool for imposing its sovereignty everywhere, and it is the guarantor of embodying the idea of true justice, which does not stop at the texts of the law alone, but finds its reflection in the actual implementation of those texts. Nevertheless, the practical application of laws is not without challenges. Practical experiences have shown the need to keep pace with developments in communication methods, especially in light of the successful implementation of "electronic notification" in notifying lawsuits and appeals in accordance with the provisions of the Civil and Commercial Procedures Law issued by Decree-Law No. 38 of 1980 . Furthermore, there are numerous problems related to the collection of criminally imposed fines, which have become a significant obstacle to achieving social and financial justice. And since the Amiri Order was issued on 10/5/2024, and Article (4) thereof stipulated that laws shall be issued by decree-laws , therefore, the present draft decree-law was prepared, and its first article stipulated the replacement of Articles (188, 230) of the Code of Criminal Procedures and Trials issued by Law No. 17 They have agreed as follows:

chunks.title: Article 1
chunks.text_en: (1) Citizens of the State of Kuwait , who hold a valid biometric diplomatic or special passport, are not required to obtain a visa to enter the territory of the Republic of Austria , and may stay for a period not exceeding 90 (ninety) days within 180 (one hundred and eighty) days, calculated from the day of first entry, either into the territory of the Republic of Austria or the territory of any other state to which the Schengen Convention of 19 June 1990, implementing the Schengen Agreement of 14 June 1985 on the gradual abolition of checks at their common borders, is applicable. (2) Citizens of the Republic of Austria , who hold a valid biometric diplomatic or service passport, are not required to obtain a visa to enter the territory of the State of Kuwait , and may stay for a period not exceeding 90 (ninety) days within 180 (one hundred and eighty) days from the date of entry.

clauses.text_en: Citizens of the State of Kuwait , who hold a valid biometric diplomatic or special passport, are not required to obtain a visa to enter the territory of the Republic of Austria , and may stay for a period not exceeding 90 (ninety) days within 180 (one hundred and eighty) days, calculated from the day of first entry, either into the territory of the Republic of Austria  or the territory of any other state to which the Schengen Convention of 19 June 1990, implementing the Schengen Agreement of 14 June 1985 on the gradual abolition of checks at their common borders, is applicable.

Citizens of the Republic of Austria , who hold a valid biometric diplomatic or service passport, are not required to obtain a visa to enter the territory of the State of Kuwait , and may stay for a period not exceeding 90 (ninety) days within 180 (one hundred and eighty) days from the date of entry.

chunks.title: Article 2
chunks.text_en: Holders of valid biometric diplomatic or service passports for the Republic of Austria and holders of valid biometric diplomatic or special passports for the State of Kuwait may enter the territory of the other Party and exit from any point authorized for this purpose by the competent immigration authorities, without any restrictions except those stipulated in the provisions of security, immigration, customs, health entry, and other provisions that may legally apply to holders of ordinary passports.

chunks.title: Article 3
chunks.text_en: The provisions of Article 1 of this Agreement do not apply to persons who plan to stay in the territory of the other Party for a period longer than that stipulated in Article 1, or who intend to obtain employment there.

chunks.title: Article 4
chunks.text_en: Citizens of either Party who enjoy privileges and immunities in accordance with international law and hold their identity card issued by the host state, do not require a visa or residence permit to stay in the territory of that Party. Decree No. 64 of 2025 Approving an Agreement between the Government of the State of Kuwait and the Austrian Federal Government regarding Visa Exemption for Holders of Diplomatic and Special Passports for the State of Kuwait and Holders of Diplomatic and Service Passports for the Republic of Austria - Having reviewed the Constitution, - And Amiri Order issued on 2 Dhu al-Qi'dah 1445 AH corresponding to 10 May 2024 AD, - And Decree No. 84 of 2024 regarding Ministerial Succession and Delegation, and its amending decrees, - And based on the presentation of the Minister of Foreign Affairs , - And after the approval of the Council of Ministers , We have decreed the following:

chunks.title: Article One
chunks.text_en: Approval of the Agreement between the Government of the State of Kuwait and the Austrian Federal Government regarding Visa Exemption for Holders of Diplomatic and Special Passports for the State of Kuwait and Holders of Diplomatic and Service Passports for the Republic of Austria , signed in New York City on 26/9/2024, the texts of which are attached to this Decree.

chunks.title: Article Two
chunks.text_en: Ministers – each within their respective jurisdiction – shall implement this Decree, and it shall be effective from the date of its publication in the Official Gazette . Amir of Kuwait Mishal Al-Ahmad Al-Jaber Al-Sabah Acting Prime Minister Fahad Yusuf Saud Al-Sabah Minister of Foreign Affairs Abdullah Ali Abdullah Al-Yahya Issued at Seif Palace on: 15 Shawwal 1446 AH Corresponding to: 13 April 2025 AD Article 5 (1) The two Parties shall exchange, through diplomatic channels, samples of passports in accordance with Article 1 of this Agreement used by either Party within 30 (thirty) days after the signing of this Agreement. Each Party shall provide the other Party with a sample of any new diplomatic, service, or special passport if it is changed at least 30 (thirty) days before the issuance of such passports. (2) Both Parties shall duly inform each other of any amendment introduced to their respective national laws and regulations concerning the issuance of passports. (3) If a national of either Party loses their valid passport referred to in Article 1 of this Agreement in the territory of the other Party, they shall inform the competent authorities of the host State. The diplomatic or consular mission shall issue a new passport or travel document to the said national and inform the competent authorities of the host State. Article 6 (1) This Agreement shall not exempt nationals of either Party from the obligation to respect the laws and regulations of the other Party concerning the entry, stay, and exit of foreigners. (2) Both Parties reserve the right to refuse entry or shorten the duration of stay of persons considered undesirable or who endanger public peace, order, health, or national security. Article 7 Either Party may temporarily suspend this Agreement for reasons related to public order, protection of national security, public health, illegal immigration, conflict of interest in the field of human rights and fundamental freedoms, or non-cooperation in the field of readmission. The other Party shall be immediately notified of the commencement of the suspension, as well as its cancellation, through diplomatic channels. Article 8 Any dispute that may arise from the application and/or interpretation of this Agreement shall be settled through diplomatic channels and through consultations or negotiations. Article 9 This Agreement may be amended by mutual written consent of both Parties at any time. Amendments shall enter into force in accordance with the procedures stipulated in Article 10 (1) of this Agreement. Article (10) (1) This Agreement shall enter into force from the date of receipt of the last written notification through diplomatic channels, by which the Parties inform each other of the completion of the constitutional procedures for its entry into force. (2) This Agreement shall remain in force for a period of five years from the date of its entry into force and shall be automatically renewed for similar periods, unless one Party notifies the other Party in writing and through diplomatic channels of its intention to terminate this Agreement 6 months prior to its expiry date. (3) The termination of this Agreement shall not affect the validity or duration of any specific agreements, projects, and activities undertaken pursuant to this Agreement. Done in the city of New York on September 26, 2024 AD, in two original copies in Arabic, German, and English, each being equally authentic. In case of divergence in interpretation, the English text shall prevail. For The Government of the State of Kuwait Abdullah Ali Al-Yahya Minister of Foreign Affairs For The Federal Government of Austria Alexander Schallenberg Federal Minister for European and International Affairs (2)Austrian nationals, who are holders of a valid biometric diplomatic or service passport, shall not be required to obtain a visa to enter the territory of the State of Kuwait for a period which does not exceed 90 (ninety) days within a 180 (one hundred and eighty)-day period counted from the date of entry.

clauses.text_en: The two Parties shall exchange, through diplomatic channels, samples of passports in accordance with Article 1 of this Agreement used by either Party within 30 (thirty) days after the signing of this Agreement. Each Party shall provide the other Party with a sample of any new diplomatic, service, or special passport if it is changed at least 30 (thirty) days before the issuance of such passports.
Both Parties shall duly inform each other of any amendment introduced to their respective national laws and regulations concerning the issuance of passports.
If a national of either Party loses their valid passport referred to in Article 1 of this Agreement in the territory of the other Party, they shall inform the competent authorities of the host State.
This Agreement shall not exempt nationals of either Party from the obligation to respect the laws and regulations of the other Party concerning the entry, stay, and exit of foreigners.
Both Parties reserve the right to refuse entry or shorten the duration of stay of persons considered undesirable or who endanger public peace, order, health, or national security.

This Agreement shall enter into force from the date of receipt of the last written notification through diplomatic channels, by which the Parties inform each other of the completion of the constitutional procedures for its entry into force.

This Agreement shall remain in force for a period of five years from the date of its entry into force and shall be automatically renewed for similar periods, unless one Party notifies the other Party in writing and through diplomatic channels of its intention to terminate this Agreement 6 months prior to its expiry date.
The termination of this Agreement shall not affect the validity or duration of any specific agreements, projects, and activities undertaken pursuant to this Agreement.

chunks.title: Article 2
chunks.text_en: Holders of a valid biometric diplomatic or service passport of the Republic of Austria and holders of a valid biometric diplomatic or special passport of the State of Kuwait may enter into and depart from the territory of the other Party at any point authorised for that purpose by the competent immigration authorities, without any restriction except for those stipulated in the security, migratory, customs, sanitary entry and other provisions which may be legally applicable to holders of such valid passports.

chunks.title: Article 3
chunks.text_en: The provisions of Article 1 of this Agreement do not apply to persons, who are planning to stay in the territory of the other Party for a period longer than the one stated in Article 1, or intending to take up employment there.

chunks.title: Article 4
chunks.text_en: Nationals of either Party enjoying privileges and immunities according to international law and who are holders of the respective ID card issued by the receiving country, do not need a visa or a residence permit to stay in or to re-enter the territory of this Party as long as this ID card is valid and presented upon entry along with a valid biometric diplomatic or service passport of the Republic of Austria or a valid biometric diplomatic or special passport of the State of Kuwait.

chunks.title: Article 5
chunks.text_en: (1)The Parties shall exchange, through diplomatic channels, samples of the passports according to Article 1 of this agreement used by either Party within 30 (thirty) days after the signing of this Agreement; and each Party shall provide to the other Party the sample of any new or changed diplomatic, service or special passport within at least 30 (thirty) days before issuing such passports. (2)Both Parties shál' duly inform each other about any modification introduced in their respective national laws and regulations related to passport issuance. (3)If a national of either Party loses his/her valid passport referred to in Article 1 of this Agreement in the territory of the other Party, he/she shall inform the competent authorities of the receiving Party. The diplomatic mission or consulate concerned shall issue a new passport or travel document to the aforementioned national and inform the competent authorities of the receiving Party.

chunks.title: Article 6
chunks.text_en: (1)This Agreement does not exempt citizens of either Party from the obligation to respect the laws and regulations of the other Party concerning the entry, stay and exit of foreigners. (2)Both Parties reserve the right to refuse admission to or shorten the stay of persons considered undesirable or endangering public peace, order, health or national security.

chunks.title: Article 7
chunks.text_en:
Either Party may suspend this Agreement, for reasons of public order, national security, public health, irregular immigration, conflicts of interests in the area of human rights and fundamental freedoms or lack of cooperation in the area of readmission. The suspension as well as its termination shall be notified immediately to the other Party through diplomatic channels. Decree No. 65 of 2025 Regarding Judicial Promotions - Having reviewed the Constitution, - And the Amiri Order issued on 2 Dhu al-Qi'dah 1445 AH, corresponding to 10 May 2024 AD, - And Decree-Law No. 14 of 1977 regarding the ranks and salaries of judges, public prosecution members, and the Fatwa and Legislation Department , and its amending laws, - And the Law on the Organization of the Judiciary issued by Decree-Law No. 23 of 1990 and its amending laws, - And Decree No. 57 of 1990 regarding the salaries and allowances of judges, public prosecution members, and the Fatwa and Legislation Department , and its amending decrees, - And Decree No. 108 of 2003 regarding the salaries and allowances of judges and public prosecution members, amended by Decree No. 23 of 2004, And Decree No. 41 of 2009 regarding the granting of a special judicial allowance to judges and public prosecution members, and its amendments by Decree No. 162 of 2011 and Decree No. 126 of 2018, - And Decree No. 84 of 2024 regarding Ministerial Succession and Delegations , and its amending decrees, - And after the approval of the Supreme Judicial Council at its session No. (2025/5) held on 9/2/2025, - And based on the submission of the Minister of Justice, - And after the approval of the Council of Ministers, We have decreed the following: Article One Promotion of Judges to Indicated Ranks Effective from 31/12/2024 | Name | Rank Promoted To | |:---|:---| | 1- Yousef Dawoud Abdullah Al-Salman | Judge of the First Grade | | 2- Fahad Naji Ibrahim Ibrahim | Judge of the First Grade | | 3- Abdulaziz Abdul Redha Yaqoub Karashi | Judge of the First Grade | | 4- Abdullah Saleh Abdullah Al-Hammadi | Judge of the First Grade |
Article Two The Minister of Justice shall implement this Decree, and it shall be published in the Official Gazette. Amir of Kuwait Mishal Al-Ahmad Al-Jaber Al-Sabah Acting Prime Minister Fahad Yousef Saud Al-Sabah Minister of Justice Counselor / Nasser Yousef Mohammed Al-Sumait Issued at Seif Palace on: 15 Shawwal 1446 AH Corresponding to: 13 April 2025 AD Article 8 Any dispute that may arise from the application and/or interpretation of this Agreement shall be settled through diplomatic channels and through consultations or negotiations. Article 9 This Agreement may be amended by the mutual written consent of both Parties at any time. The amendments shall enter into force in accordance with the procedures prescribed in Article 10 (1) of this Agreement. Article 10 (1)This Agreement shall enter into force on the date of the receipt of the last written notification through diplomatic channels by which the Parties notify each other of the completion of their constitutional procedures for its entry into force. (2)This Agreement shall remain in force for a period of 5 years from the date of its entry into force and shall be renewed automatically for similar period or periods, unless one of the Parties notifies the other in writing and through diplomatic channels about its intention to terminate it 6 months before its expiration date. (3)The termination of the Agreement shall not affect the validity or the duration of any specific agreements, projects and activities made under this Agreement. DONE in New York City on 26September 2024, in two originals, each in English, Arabic and German language, all texts being equally authentic. In case of discrepancies in interpretation of the provisions of the present agreement, the English text shall prevail. For the Government of the State of Kuwait Abdullah Ali Abdullah Al-Yahya Minister of Foreign Affairs For the Austrian Federal Government Alexander Schallenberg Federal Minister for European and International Affairs

chunks.title: Regarding the Formation of the Board of Commissioners of the Capital Markets Authority <orig>هيئة أسواق المال</orig>
chunks.text_en: - Having reviewed the Constitution, - And the Amiri Order issued on 2 Dhu al-Qi'dah 1445 AH, corresponding to 10 May 2024 AD, - And Law No. 7 of 2010 regarding the establishment of the Capital Markets Authority and the regulation of securities, and its amending laws, - And Decree No. 235 of 2018 regarding the formation of the Board of Commissioners of the Capital Markets Authority , - And Decree No. 219 of 2019 regarding the appointment of a member to the Board of Commissioners of the Capital Markets Authority , - And Decree No. 84 of 2024 regarding Ministerial Succession and Delegations , and its amending decrees, - And based on the submission of the Minister of Commerce and Industry, - And after the approval of the Council of Ministers, We have decreed the following: Article One The Board of Commissioners of the Capital Markets Authority shall be formed for a period of four years, renewable for one term, as follows: 1- Emad Ahmed Tifouni (Chairman) 2- Rayan Mohammed Al-Zaid (Vice Chairman) 3- Tariq Abdul Latif Ali Al-Shehab (Member) 4- Hessa Abdulaziz Al-Roumi (Member) 5- Thamer Nabil Al-Nisf (Member) Article Two The Minister of Commerce and Industry shall implement this Decree, and it shall be effective from the date of its issuance, and published in the Official Gazette.

chunks.title: Appointing Two Non-Resident Ambassadors
chunks.text_en: - After reviewing the Constitution, - And on the Amiri Order issued on 2 Dhu al-Qa'dah 1445 AH corresponding to 10 May 2024 AD, - And on Law No. 21 of 1962 regarding the Diplomatic and Consular Corps System and its amending laws, - And on Decree No. 84 of 2024 concerning Ministerial Substitutions and Delegations , and its amending decrees, - And based on the proposal of the Minister of Foreign Affairs , - And after the approval of the Council of Ministers , We have decreed the following: Article One Each of the following shall be appointed: 1- Reem Mohammed Al-Khaled - Ambassador of the State of Kuwait to the Federal Republic of Germany - in addition to her current duties - as non-resident Ambassador of the State of Kuwait to the Republic of Latvia. 2- Sabah Nasser Al-Malik Al-Sabah - Ambassador of the State of Kuwait to New Zealand - in addition to his current duties - as non-resident Ambassador of the State of Kuwait to the Cook Islands. Article Two The Minister of Foreign Affairs shall implement this Decree, and it shall be effective from the date of its issuance and published in the Official Gazette . <footer> Amir of Kuwait Mishal Al-Ahmad Al-Jaber Al-Sabah Acting Prime Minister Fahad Yousef Saud Al-Sabah Minister of Commerce and Industry Khalifa Abdullah Dhahi Al-Ajeel Al-Askar Minister of Foreign Affairs Abdullah Ali Abdullah Al-Yahya Issued at Seif Palace on: 15 Shawwal 1446 AH Corresponding to: 13 April 2025 AD </footer>

chunks.title: Under the Name (Akhbar Bouali <orig>أخبار بوعلي</orig>)
chunks.text_en: Assistant Undersecretary for the Press, Publishing and Publications Sector Having reviewed, - Law No. 8 of 2016 regarding the Regulation of Electronic Media. - Ministerial Decision No. 100 of 2016 issuing the Executive Regulations for Law No. 8 of 2016 regulating Electronic Media. - Administrative Decision No. 655 of 2022 regarding the Delegation of Certain Competencies. - Electronic Media License No. 2017/152 issued on: 2017/08/10. - The request submitted by the licensee on 2025/04/08. - The letter from the Director of the Electronic Publishing Department No.: 20 dated: 2025/04/10. - And in accordance with the public interest. Has Decided (Article One) License No. 2017/152 for the electronic media outlet (Akhbar Bouali ) shall be cancelled by operation of law, based on the request of the licensee, pursuant to the last paragraph, item 8 of Article 16 of the aforementioned Law No. 8 of 2016 regulating Electronic Media. (Article Two) - The competent authorities shall implement this decision, and it shall be published on the Ministry's website and in the Official Gazette. Assistant Undersecretary for the Press, Publishing and Publications Sector Issued on: 15 Shawwal 1446 AH Corresponding to: 13 April 2025 AD

chunks.title: Appointing a Non-Resident Ambassador
chunks.text_en: Having reviewed the Constitution, - And Amiri Order issued on 2 Dhu al-Qi'dah 1445 AH corresponding to 10 May 2024 AD, - And Law No. 21 of 1962 regarding the Diplomatic and Consular Corps System and its amending laws, - And Decree No. 84 of 2024 concerning Ministerial Succession and Delegations and its amending decrees, - And based on the proposal of the Minister of Foreign Affairs, - And after the approval of the Council of Ministers, We have decreed the following: Article One Appointment of / Reem Mohammad Al-Khaled – Ambassador of the State of Kuwait to the Federal Republic of Germany – in addition to her current duties – as a non-resident Ambassador of the State of Kuwait to the Republic of Lithuania. Article Two The Minister of Foreign Affairs shall implement this Decree, and it shall be effective from its date of issuance and published in the Official Gazette. Amir of Kuwait Mishal Al-Ahmad Al-Jaber Al-Sabah Acting Prime Minister Fahad Yusuf Saud Al-Sabah Minister of Foreign Affairs Abdullah Ali Abdullah Al-Yahya Issued at Seif Palace on: 16 Shawwal 1446 AH Corresponding to: 14 April 2025 AD

chunks.title: Regarding the Cancellation of the License for (Kuwait Post <orig>كويت بوست</orig>) Magazine
chunks.text_en: Assistant Undersecretary of the Ministry of Information for Press, Publishing and Publications Affairs Having reviewed: * Law No. 3 of 2006 concerning Publications and Publishing, and its amendments. * And on Ministerial Decision No. 52 of 2006 regarding the Executive Regulations of the Press and Publications Law and its amendments, * And on the letter from the Gulf Press Department No. 177 dated 10/4/2025, which includes the licensee's request. Decided

chunks.title: Article One
chunks.text_en: The license issued for (Kuwait Post) Magazine is hereby cancelled, based on the request of the licensee, issued on 11/11/2008, in accordance with the last paragraph of Article (14) of Law No. 3 of 2006 referred to.

chunks.title: Article Two
chunks.text_en: The competent authorities shall implement this decision, and it shall be published in the Official Gazette , and concerned parties shall be notified by registered mail with acknowledgment of receipt. Assistant Undersecretary for Press, Publishing, and Publications Affairs Issued on: 16 Shawwal 1446 AH Corresponding to: 14 April 2025 AD

chunks.title: Regarding the Resignation of the Undersecretary
chunks.text_en: Minister of Commerce and Industry Having reviewed: * Decree-Law No. (15) of 1979 regarding Civil Service , and its amending laws, * And on the Decree issued on 4 April 1979 regarding the Civil Service System , and its amending decrees, * And on Decree No. (191) of 2015 regarding the Organization of the Ministry of Commerce and Industry , * And on Ministerial Decision No. (152) of 2011 regarding the Organizational Structure of the Ministry of Commerce and Industry , * And on Decree No. 152 of 2023 appointing Mr. Ziyad Abdullah Al-Najem as Undersecretary of the Ministry of Commerce and Industry , * And on Decree No. 73 of 2024 regarding the Formation of the Cabinet and its amending decrees, * And on the resignation letter dated 25/3/2025 submitted by the Undersecretary, Mr. Ziyad Abdullah Al-Najem , based on his desire, * And on the letter from the General Secretariat of the Council of Ministers dated 9/4/2025 regarding informing the Council of Ministers of the acceptance of the resignation of the Undersecretary, Mr. Ziyad Abdullah Al-Najem , * And based on the requirements of public interest, * And pursuant to the powers vested in us. Decided

chunks.title: Article One
chunks.text_en: The resignation of Mr. Ziyad Abdullah Al-Najem (Civil ID: 279101100036) from the position of Undersecretary of the Ministry of Commerce and Industry is accepted, effective from the end of the workday on 25/3/2025.

chunks.title: Article Two
chunks.text_en: All concerned parties shall be notified of this decision, and necessary measures shall be taken for its implementation.

chunks.title: Article Three
chunks.text_en: All officials – each within their respective jurisdiction – shall implement this decision, which shall be effective from its date of issuance and published in the Official Gazette . chunks.title: Regarding Additional Capacity Subscriptions for Internet Service Providers via Fiber Optic Network
chunks.text_en: The Minister of State for Communications Affairs : - After reviewing Article (130) of the Constitution; - And Law No. (37) of 2014 establishing the Communications and Information Technology Regulatory Authority and its amendment; - And Decree-Law No. (1) of 2025 concerning Fees and Financial Costs for the Utilization of Public Facilities and Services; - And the Amiri Decree issued on 12/8/1986 concerning the competencies of the Ministry of Communications and its amendments; - And Ministerial Resolution No. (329) of 2012 regarding Internet Service Tariffs via Fiber Optic Network; - And Ministerial Resolution No. (347) of 2016 regarding Internet Service Tariffs via Fiber Optic Network; - And the report of the Communications and Information Technology Regulatory Authority regarding the stimulation of Internet services via Fiber Optic Network (GPON); - And the presentation by Mr./ Undersecretary of the Ministry of Communications in letter No. (W.N.S./15) dated 26/1/2025; - And Council of Ministers Resolution No. (375) adopted at its meeting No. (2025/12)-(47) held on 26/3/2025 regarding the approval of the proposed Ministerial Resolution prepared by His Excellency the Minister of State for Communications Affairs concerning additional capacity subscriptions for Internet service providers via fiber optic network. Has Decided

chunks.title: Article One
chunks.text_en: For the purposes of this Resolution, the following terms shall have the meanings ascribed to them below: Definitions of Terms Related to Internet Service Capacity | Term | Meaning | |:--------|:------| | Service Provider: | An Internet service provider licensed by the Communications and Information Technology Regulatory Authority to provide Internet services. | | Existing Capacities: | The total registered capacity subscriptions for each service provider at the time of issuance of this Resolution. | | Additional Capacities: | Capacities allocated to the service provider in addition to the existing capacities, at a rate of (1 Gbps) for each capacity allocated in accordance with the provisions of this Resolution. |

chunks.title: Article Two
chunks.text_en: The provisions of this Resolution shall apply only to additional capacity subscriptions allocated to service providers starting from the effective date of this Resolution. The tariff stipulated in the aforementioned Ministerial Resolution No. (347) of 2016 shall continue to apply to existing capacity subscriptions.

chunks.title: Article Three
chunks.text_en: Without prejudice to the tariff stipulated in Article One of the aforementioned Resolution (347) of 2016, the allocation value for additional capacity subscriptions for a (1 Gbps) capacity shall be (12,000) (twelve thousand Kuwaiti Dinars) K.D. annually.

chunks.title: Article Four
chunks.text_en: The allocation period for the additional capacity mentioned in Article Three of this Resolution shall be one full calendar year, and its allocation fee shall be paid in advance to the Ministry of Communications , in addition to installation charges. The allocation value shall be non-refundable.

chunks.title: Article Five
chunks.text_en: For the application of the tariff mentioned in Article Three of this Resolution, it is a condition that the service provider continues to renew and does not reduce the existing capacity subscriptions for each system separately (NGN-A / NGN-B).

chunks.title: Article Six
chunks.text_en: Should the service provider reduce below the existing capacities during the period of benefiting from the allocation according to the value mentioned in Article Three of this Resolution, the following effects shall ensue: 1. The provision of Article Three of this Resolution shall not apply to the service provider. 2. The value stipulated in the aforementioned Ministerial Resolution No. (347) of 2016 shall apply to all capacities. The date of reduction below the existing capacities shall be the one adopted when conducting financial settlement.

clauses.text_en: The provision of Article Three of this Resolution shall not apply to the service provider.
The value stipulated in the aforementioned Ministerial Resolution No. (347) of 2016 shall apply to all capacities.

chunks.title: Article Seven
chunks.text_en: The provisions of the aforementioned Ministerial Resolution No. (347) of 2016 shall apply to matters not specifically regulated in this Resolution.

chunks.title: Article Eight
chunks.text_en: This Resolution shall be published in the Official Gazette and shall be effective from the date of its publication for a period of one year. <footer> Minister of State for Communications Affairs Omar Saud Abdulaziz Al-Omar Issued on: 14 April 2025 AD </footer> Capital Markets Authority

chunks.title: Decision No. (60) of 2025
chunks.text_en: Regarding the License Application Submitted by Rasameel Investment Company to Practice Securities Activities Having reviewed: - Law No. (7) of 2010 Regarding the Establishment of the Capital Markets Authority and the Regulation of Securities Activities, its Executive Regulations, and their amendments; - And the application for a license for securities activities submitted by Rasameel Investment Company ; - And based on Decision No. (4) of 2025 Regarding the Amendment of Final Approval Powers for the Work Procedures of the Supervision Sector, issued on 12/01/2025. Has decided the following:

chunks.title: Article One
chunks.text_en: The Authority grants preliminary approval to Rasameel Investment Company for licensing the activity of a securities broker not registered on the stock exchange. This approval is valid for a period of six months, renewable, starting from 16/04/2025 and ending on 15/10/2025, for the purpose of fulfilling the criteria specified by the Authority.

chunks.title: Article Two
chunks.text_en: The company mentioned in this Decision shall be licensed, after completing the requirements referred to in the preliminary approval letter issued by the Authority, for a period of three years, renewable, from the date of issuance of the Authority's license certificate and after payment of the prescribed fee.

chunks.title: Article Three
chunks.text_en: The competent authorities shall implement this Decision, each within its respective jurisdiction. It shall be effective from the date of its issuance and shall be published in the Official Gazette . Issued on: 16 April 2025 AD

chunks.title: Announcement
chunks.text_en: The Ministry of Information announces the tender for Practice No. 915/2025-2026 regarding the supply and installation of repairs for the Government Press at the Ministry of Information , in accordance with the terms and technical specifications contained in the practice documents, which can be purchased via the Ministry of Information's website using the barcode attached to the announcement, effective from Sunday, 2025/4/20, for a fee of (50 KD). K) only fifty Kuwaiti Dinars only, non-refundable. Bids are to be submitted to the Procurement Department at the Ministry of Information - Al-Sour Street - Ground Floor, inside sealed envelopes, accompanied by an initial security deposit of (2%) of the bid value (which must be valid throughout the bid validity period of 90 days). The envelope must be sealed and the practice number, as indicated above, must be written on the outside, while avoiding any mention of the bidder's name or any indication thereof. The final deadline for bid submission is 1:00 PM on Thursday, 2025/5/8. * The Ministry of Information will hold a preliminary meeting on Sunday, 2025/4/27, at 11:00 AM at the office of Mr./Director of the Government Press - Ministry of Information 2 in Al-Shuwaikh , to answer inquiries regarding the practice. Anyone who has purchased the practice documents may attend the meeting in person or through a representative. Everything recorded in the meeting minutes shall be considered an integral part of the practice documents and shall be binding on bidders. The Ministry will circulate the questions, inquiries, and their approved responses to all bidders before the bid closing date. * All documents must be submitted on a non-copyable CD (3 copies), with the exception of (the bid form, initial security deposit, and a copy of the purchase receipt, which must be submitted as hard copies). * The practice is indivisible, and alternative offers will not be accepted. * This announcement serves as an invitation for bidders wishing to attend the bid opening session, after coordination with the Procurement Department before the scheduled date for the bid opening session. * A valid National Labor Fulfillment Certificate issued by the Public Authority for Manpower must be submitted with the bid. Bids that do not include this certificate will be excluded. * The bidder must attach a valid 'To Whom It May Concern' certificate issued by the Public Authority for Manpower stating that there is no suspension on the bidder's file regarding government contracts mentioned in Article 10 of Law No. 6 of 2010 concerning work in the private sector. Bids that do not include this certificate will be excluded. * Owners of Small and Medium Enterprises are exempt from the prescribed fees for purchasing documents, in accordance with the text of Article 39 of Law No. 74 of 2019 regarding amendments to Law No. 49 of 2016 . * Owners of Small and Medium Enterprises wishing to bid for the practice must attach a valid certificate issued by the National Fund for Small and Medium Enterprise Care and Development , confirming their accreditation as a small or medium enterprise. * This announcement is considered an integral part of the practice documents. * To obtain a copy, the bidder must register and pay fees through the website: /https://eservice.media.gov.kw/purchase: <image_caption>QR Code for Ministry of Information Purchase Portal</image_caption> <image> { "description": "QR code linking to the Ministry of Information's e-service purchase portal.", "type": "qr_code", "data": "https://eservice.media.gov.kw/purchase" } </image>

chunks.title: Correction
chunks.text_en: A material error occurred in the Ministry of Commerce and Industry's announcement regarding M.S. Tex International for Ready-Made Garments ## Company Under Liquidation in Issue 1734, published on Sunday, 2025/4/13 AD, where an error appeared in the company's name. Therefore, we are re-publishing the announcement after correcting the error.

chunks.title: Announcement
chunks.text_en: The owners of M.S. Tex International for Ready-Made Garments ## Company Under Liquidation have submitted a request to the Department of Partnerships at the Ministry to carry out/ dissolve and liquidate the company. Legal Entity: One-Person Company . Anyone with an objection is requested to submit it to the General Court within thirty days from the date of publication of this announcement, in accordance with the usual procedures for filing a lawsuit. The submission of an objection results in the continued obligation of the joint partners, in the case of a joint liability company or a simple limited partnership only, towards this creditor, until their objection is decided by a final judgment.

chunks.title: Announcement
chunks.text_en: Regarding the Offering of Auction No. (DA/134/2024-2025) for Licensing the Exploitation, Management, and Operation of an Investment Site for Providing Fresh Juices (Local) at Al-Shaheed Park , Phase Three – Site (H2) The Amiri Diwan announces the offering of Auction No. (DA/134/2024-2025) for licensing the exploitation, management, and operation of an investment site for providing fresh juices (local) at Al-Shaheed Park , Phase Three – Site No. (H2). Those wishing to participate, from companies or institutions specialized in this field, should apply to obtain the terms and conditions booklet and auction documents during official working hours from (Amiri Diwan – Bayyan Palace – Administrative Building – First Floor – Zone 7 – Tenders and Follow-up Department) during official working hours, starting from the date of publication of this announcement in the Official Gazette "Al-Kuwait Al-Youm ", for a non-refundable fee of (75/- K.D.) seventy-five Kuwaiti Dinars only (K-Net only payment) for the booklet. Owners of Small and Medium Enterprises are exempted from these fees (upon submission of the relevant certificate). This is effective from Sunday, 20/4/2025, from 8:30 AM until 1:00 PM, taking into account the following: 1- The deadline for submitting bids is 1:00 PM on Thursday, 1/5/2025. No bids will be accepted after this time. 2- Bids must be submitted in a sealed envelope to the Tenders and Follow-up Department – at Bayyan Palace – Administrative Building – First Floor – Zone 7. 3- Submission of an initial security deposit of not less than (2%) of the bid value in favor of the Amiri Diwan in the form of a letter of guarantee or a certified check from one of the Kuwaiti banks, valid throughout the bid validity period of (90) days. 4- Submission of a valid certificate of compliance with the national labor percentage. 5- A letter of authorization and a copy of the Civil ID for the company representative to purchase the auction document. 6- This auction is indivisible, and alternative offers are not permitted. 7- The bidder must submit a letter accompanied by a copy of the registration certificate from the Kuwait Chamber of Commerce and Industry . 8- The site inspection and preliminary meeting will be held on Sunday, 27/4/2025, at 10:00 AM at Al-Shaheed Park , Phase Three. 9- The bid must remain valid for a period of (90) days from the closing date, with the Amiri Diwan reserving the right to extend or renew this period for such duration(s) as it deems appropriate to complete the award procedures. 10- Must be registered in the Companies Register at the Amiri Diwan (Amiri Diwan website) https://pnsd.da.gov.kw/ar The bidding is subject to Circular No. (1) of 2023 regarding Bidding in Public Entities . For inquiries: Email: mw.alsaleh@da.gov.kw Direct: 22011432 Undersecretary for Financial, Administrative, and Staff Affairs

chunks.title: Announcement
chunks.text_en: The Diwan of His Highness the Crown Prince announces the offering of Practice No. (1 / 2025 - 2026) regarding the renewal of device licenses at the Diwan of His Highness the Crown Prince (divisible). Specialized companies / establishments in this field are invited to review the Equipment and Contracts Department (Contracts and Tenders Section) at the Diwan of His Highness the Crown Prince (Bayan Palace – Administrative Building – Third Floor – ZONE 5) to obtain the documents for this practice for a fee of (50 K.D.) only fifty Kuwaiti Dinars , payable via (K-Net). Owners of small and medium enterprises are exempt from these fees, provided they present proof, along with a copy of the practice announcement in the Official Gazette (Kuwait Today / Al-Kuwait Al-Youm ) and the following documents: 9- A valid certificate from the Public Authority for Manpower regarding the percentage of national labor. 10- A letter of authorization from the company / establishment for the person authorized to purchase the practice documents. Please note that the preliminary meeting for receiving any inquiries is on Monday, 28/04/2025, at 11:00 AM, and the closing date (last date for submitting bids) is on Monday, 05/05/2025, from 9:00 AM until 1:00 PM. The bid must be accompanied by a preliminary bond in the form of a certified check or a letter of guarantee issued by a bank accredited in the State of Kuwait in the name of the bidder and in favor of the Diwan of His Highness the Crown Prince for an amount of (1,400 K.D.) only one thousand four hundred Kuwaiti Dinars . The preliminary bond and the bid must remain valid for (90) days from the date of bid opening. No bids will be accepted if not accompanied by the full bond or if submitted after the aforementioned deadline. For inquiries: 1853333 ext. 4401 – 4416 – 4415. Undersecretary for Financial Affairs and Equipment

The preliminary guarantee amounts to 4,555/- K.D. of the bid value, provided it is valid throughout the bid validity period and issued in the name of the General Secretariat of the Council of Ministers . 1. A copy of the company's articles of association (if any). 5. A copy of the valid registration certificate from the Kuwait Chamber of Commerce and Industry . 6. A copy of the valid registration receipt from the Central Agency for Public Tenders . 7. A copy of the valid registration receipt from the Central Agency for Information Technology . 8. A copy of the practitioner's authorized signature. Please bring the following documents to obtain the terms and conditions booklet: 1. Valid registration certificate with the Central Agency for Public Tenders . 2. Valid registration certificate with the Central Agency for Information Technology . 3. Valid certificate from the Public Authority for Civil Information (Civil Entity Number). 4. Valid national labor percentage fulfillment certificate. 5. Authorization letter and a copy of the civil ID for the company's representative to purchase the booklet. chunks.title: Kuwait Credit Bank <orig>بنك الائتمان الكويتي</orig> Announcement
chunks.text_en: Kuwait Credit Bank announces the offering of Auction No. (01-2025/2026) regarding the utilization and management of the first, second, third, fourth, and fifth floors with furniture in the Kuwait Credit Bank building - located in Al-Qibla area, according to the terms and specifications stated in the auction documents, which can be obtained from the Bank, Surra - Financial Department - Sixth Floor. Therefore, companies wishing to participate in the auction may obtain the general terms and specifications booklet during the following hours: from (8:00 AM until 1:00 PM) for a non-refundable fee of (K.D. 75/-) seventy-five Kuwaiti Dinars , provided they bring the following documents: 1. A copy of a valid Commercial Register. 2. A copy of a valid Kuwait Chamber of Commerce and Industry certificate. 3. The bidder must have a valid license issued by Kuwait Municipality . 4. The bidder must submit a detailed record of the works undertaken by them relevant to the requirements, supported by authenticated certificates from the concerned authorities. 5. The bidder must have a qualified team with professional experience and full knowledge of the nature of the required work. 6. A letter of authorization from the company to purchase the auction documents, along with the name(s) of the authorized signatory or those authorized to contract with the Bank, and samples of their signatures. 7. The bid must be accompanied by a preliminary bond of K.D. 2000, which shall be in the form of a certified check or a letter of guarantee from one of the accredited banks in Kuwait in favor of Kuwait Credit Bank , unconditional and valid for the duration of the bid. 8. The site visit will be held at the Bank's building located in Al-Qibla area - on Thursday, 2025/05/08, from 10:00 AM to 11:00 AM. 9. A preliminary meeting will be held at the Bank's main building - South Surra - Fifth Floor, Meeting Room, on Sunday, 2025/05/11, from 10:00 AM to 11:00 AM. 10. Bids must be submitted in a sealed envelope addressed to Kuwait Credit Bank and deposited in the Bank's Auction Committee box at the Financial Department of Kuwait Credit Bank (South Surra ) no later than 1:00 PM on Thursday, 2025/05/15.

chunks.title: Announcement
chunks.text_en: The Ministry of Information announces the issuance of Practice No.: 914 for the year 2025-2026 concerning the supply, installation, inspection, delivery, warranty, training, and provision of radio messaging emergency devices and system for the Ministry of Information , in accordance with the terms and technical specifications contained in the practice documents, which can be purchased from the Ministry of Information's website via the barcode attached to the announcement, starting from Sunday, 20/04/2025, for a non-refundable fee of only (K.D. 50/-) fifty Kuwaiti Dinars . Offers must be submitted to the Procurement Department at the Ministry of Information in sealed envelopes, accompanied by an initial security deposit of (2%) of the bid value (which must be valid throughout the bid validity period of 90 days). The practice number, as indicated above, must be written on the outside of the envelope, avoiding any mention of the practitioner's name or any indication thereof. The final deadline for bid submission is 1:00 PM on Sunday, 18/05/2025. * The Ministry of Information will hold a preliminary meeting on Tuesday, 06/05/2025, at 10:00 AM at the office of Mr. / Assistant Undersecretary for Engineering Affairs - Media Complex - Al-Sour Street - Ground Floor, to answer inquiries regarding the practice. Those who have purchased the practice documents may attend the meeting in person or through a representative. Everything recorded in the meeting minutes shall be considered an integral part of the practice documents and shall be binding on bidders. The Ministry will circulate the questions, inquiries, and their approved responses to all practitioners before the bid closing date. * All documents must be submitted on a non-copyable CD, 3 copies, with the exception of (the bid form, initial security deposit, a copy of the purchase receipt, and the practitioner's data form). * The practice is indivisible, and alternative offers will not be accepted. * This announcement serves as an invitation for practitioners wishing to attend the bid opening session, after coordination with the Procurement Department before the scheduled date for the bid opening session. * A valid National Labor Compliance Certificate issued by the Public Authority for Manpower must be submitted with the bid. Bids that do not include this certificate will be excluded. * The bidder must attach a valid "To Whom It May Concern" certificate issued by the Public Authority for Manpower stating that there is no suspension on the practitioner's file regarding government contracts registered with the Authority due to any of the violations mentioned in Article 10 of Law No. 6 of 2010 concerning work in the private sector. Bids that do not include this certificate will be excluded. * Owners of Small and Medium Enterprises are exempt from the prescribed fees for purchasing documents, in accordance with the text of Article 39 of Law No. 74 of 2019 regarding amendments to Law No. 49 of 2016 . * Owners of Small and Medium Enterprises wishing to apply for the practice must attach a valid certificate issued by the National Fund for Small and Medium Enterprise Care and Development confirming their accreditation as a small or medium enterprise with the fund. * This announcement is considered an integral part of the practice documents. * To obtain a copy, the practitioner must register and pay the fees through the website https://eservice.media.gov.kw/purchase <image_caption>QR Code for Ministry of Information E-Service</image_caption> <image> { "description": "QR code linking to the Ministry of Information's e-service for purchasing tender documents.", "type": "qr_code", "data": "https://eservice.media.gov.kw/purchase" } </image>

K.S.C. (Closed), at the company's headquarters located in the Ministries Area, Block 1, in the main meeting hall of the Public Services Building, 3rd Floor. The esteemed shareholders at the meeting approved that Mr. Anwar Talal Al-Fadhli – Vice Chairman of the Board of Directors – would chair the meeting session. Also present at the meeting were: - Ms. Afrah Ali Al-Sharafi – Board Secretary – (Meeting Secretary). - Shareholders holding 139,337,419 shares (in person and by proxy), representing 99.998% of the total paid-up capital shares of the company, which number 139,347,000 shares. After the legal quorum for the validity of the Extraordinary General Assembly meeting was announced, Mr. Anwar Talal Al-Fadhli – Chairman of the meeting session – opened the meeting by welcoming the esteemed attendees, then proceeded to present the items on the agenda of the Extraordinary General Assembly meeting for discussion, and the following was accomplished:- Item One: The amendment of Article (13) of the Articles of Association was approved as follows: - Current Text: The company shall be managed by a Board of Directors consisting of five members, elected by the General Assembly by secret ballot, and any shareholder, whether a natural or legal person, may appoint representatives to the company's Board of Directors in proportion to their shareholding therein, and the number of Board members selected in this manner shall be deducted from the total number of Board members to be elected, and shareholders who have representatives on the Board of Directors may not participate with other shareholders in the election of the remaining Board members, except to the extent exceeding the percentage used in appointing their representatives to the Board of Directors, and a group of shareholders may form an alliance among themselves to appoint one or more representatives for them on the Board of Directors in proportion to their combined ownership, and these representatives shall have the rights and duties of elected members, and the shareholder shall be responsible for the actions of their representatives towards the company, its entity, and its shareholders. Amounts due for representation on the company's Board of Directors shall accrue to public institutions, public bodies, and wholly state-owned companies, and the Chairman of the Board of Directors must pay these amounts directly to those entities within one week from their due date, and the aforementioned entities may determine the remunerations and salaries they disburse to their representatives on the company's Board of Directors. Membership in the company's Board of Directors shall not be included within the maximum number of memberships, and a person may be the chairman of the board of directors of more than one closed shareholding company, and the company may have a Chief Executive Officer from among the Board members or from outside, and Board members shall be elected by secret ballot at the General Assembly meeting, and a Board member may dispose of their shares in the company during their membership on the Board, without prejudice to the restrictions on share disposal stipulated in Companies Law No. 25 of 2012 , its amendments, and its executive regulations. - Text After Amendment: The company shall be managed by a Board of Directors consisting of (3) members, from whom the entity or entities entitled to appoint representatives to the Board of Directors shall appoint members in proportion to their ownership in the company's capital, and the remaining members shall be elected by secret ballot. Finally, the Chairman of the meeting thanked the esteemed attendees, and accordingly, the proceedings of the Extraordinary General Assembly meeting concluded at 12:00 AM on the same day. Chairman of the Board of Directors Item Two: Approval of the Board of Directors' proposal to increase the company's authorized, issued, and paid-up capital in kind, in accordance with the asset valuation report prepared by Messrs./ BDO Economic, Administrative, Industrial, and Computer Consulting and Opening Private Training Institutes (L.L.C.) , so that the increase in the company's authorized, issued, and paid-up capital shall be from KWD 2,100,000 (Two Million One Hundred Thousand Kuwaiti Dinars) distributed over 21,000,000 shares, with a par value of 100 fils per share, to KWD 23,286,942 (Twenty-Three Million Two Hundred Eighty-Six Thousand Nine Hundred Forty-Two Kuwaiti Dinars) distributed over 232,869,420 shares, with a par value of 100 fils per share, with an increase of KWD 21,186,942 (Twenty-One Million One Hundred Eighty-Six Thousand Nine Hundred Forty-Two Kuwaiti Dinars) distributed over 211,869,420 shares, with a par value of 100 fils per share, allocated to existing shareholders. Approved unanimously Item Three: Approval of the asset valuation report prepared by Messrs./ BDO Economic, Administrative, Industrial, and Computer Consulting and Opening Private Training Institutes (L.L.C.) which showed the fair value of partners' shares in the companies as follows: - KWD 2,079,732 value of 100% of partners' shares in Al-Othman and Al-Bisher Trading Company . - KWD 19,321,219 value of 100% of partners' shares in Ajial Contracting and General Trading Company . And the transfer of ownership of 99% of the partners' shares in Ajial Contracting and General Trading Company and Al-Othman and Al-Bisher Trading Company with a value of KWD 21,186,942 in favor of Ajial Holding Company . Approved unanimously Item Four: Amendment of Article No. (8) of the Company's Memorandum of Association and Articles of Association as follows: - Article Before Amendment: The company's authorized, issued, and paid-up capital was set at KWD 2,100,000 (Two Million One Hundred Thousand Kuwaiti Dinars only) distributed over 21,000,000 shares, with a par value of 100 fils per share, and all shares are cash shares. - Article After Amendment: The company's authorized, issued, and paid-up capital was set at KWD 23,286,942 (Twenty-Three Million Two Hundred Eighty-Six Thousand Nine Hundred Forty-Two Kuwaiti Dinars) distributed over 232,869,42 <header>

chunks.title: Minutes of the Extraordinary General Assembly Meeting
chunks.text_en: On Monday, March 24, 2025, at 11:30 AM, the shareholders of Al-Ra'eda for Petrochemicals Company – a Kuwaiti Shareholding Company (Closed) – convened under the chairmanship of Mr. Hussein Fahad Al-Fahad – Chairman of the Board of Directors, at the Kuwait Industrial Bank building – 10th Floor. In addition to the shareholders, the following gentlemen attended the session: * The Ministry of Commerce and Industry was notified of the date of the company's General Assembly meeting, and the Ministry confirmed its awareness of the meeting date by virtue of the extract attached to these minutes. * The Kuwait Clearing Company apologized for not attending. After the quorum was met with the attendance of 2,500,000 shares, in person and by proxy, representing 100% of the company's 2,500,000 shares with a capital of KWD 250,000, the discussion of the agenda items commenced. The Extraordinary General Assembly unanimously approved these items as follows: 1. The future of the company was discussed as follows: * The capital will be increased by KWD 250,000 in cash. * The benevolent loan will be converted into capital with a value of KWD 153,000. * Creditor balances and other credit balances amounting to KWD 9,805,990 will be written off. * The legal reserve of KWD 2,362 will be utilized to amortize part of the accumulated losses. * The amortization of accumulated losses amounting to KWD 416,967 was discussed and proposed, which will lead to a reduction of capital to KWD 250,000 after the amortization of losses. The amortization of accumulated losses reflects a fundamental step to correct the company's financial position and enable it to resume its operations without the burden of previous losses. 2. The increase of the company's authorized, issued, and paid-up capital from KWD 250,000 (Two Hundred and Fifty Thousand Kuwaiti Dinars) to KWD 653,000 (Six Hundred and Fifty-Three Thousand Kuwaiti Dinars) by an amount of KWD 403,000 (Four Hundred and Three Thousand Kuwaiti Dinars), with a share value of 100 Kuwaiti Fils, was approved through the following: a. A cash increase of KWD 250,000 (Two Hundred and Fifty Thousand Kuwaiti Dinars) allocated to current shareholders registered in the company's books up to the date of the General Assembly meeting, and authorizing the Board of Directors to set controls and conditions for capital calls and the disposal of fractional shares. b. An increase through the creditors' account of KWD 153,000 (One Hundred and Fifty-Three Thousand Kuwaiti Dinars), with the principal debt being cash, allocated to one of the company's shareholders, namely Kuwait Industrial Projects Company . 3. The amendment of Article (5) of the Articles of Association and Article (6) of the Memorandum of Incorporation was approved to read as follows: **Text Before Amendment:** "The company's capital is set at KWD 250,000 (Two Hundred and Fifty Thousand Kuwaiti Dinars), distributed over 2,500,000 shares (Two Million Five Hundred Thousand Shares), with each share valued at 100 Kuwaiti Fils, and all shares are cash shares." **Text After Amendment:** "The company's authorized and issued capital is set at KWD 653,000 (Six Hundred and Fifty-Three Thousand Kuwaiti Dinars), distributed over 6,530,000 shares (Six Million Five Hundred and Thirty Thousand Shares), with each share valued at 100 Kuwaiti Fils, and all shares are cash shares." "The company's paid-up capital is set at KWD 403,000 (Four Hundred and Three Thousand Kuwaiti Dinars), distributed over 4,030,000 shares (Four Million Thirty Thousand Shares), with each share valued at 100 Kuwaiti Fils, and all shares are cash shares." This is subject to the approval of the regulatory authorities . The meeting concluded at 12:30 PM on the same date of its convening. Chairman of the Board of Directors / Chairman of the Session

chunks.title: Kuwaiti Shareholding Company (Closed) <orig>شركة مساهمة كويتية (مقفلة)</orig> Minutes of the Extraordinary General Assembly Meeting Held on Thursday, February 19, 2025
chunks.text_en:
The Extraordinary General Assembly of the shareholders of Al-Qurain for Car Trading Company , a Kuwaiti Shareholding Company (Closed) , held its extraordinary meeting at 10:00 AM on Wednesday, February 19, 2025, at the company's headquarters located in Old Shuwaikh area, Third Street, Plot No. 16, opposite the new Kuwait Finance House showroom. The meeting was held under the chairmanship of Mr. Ahmed Abdulaziz Ali Al-Mutawa – Chairman of the Board of Directors and Chief Executive Officer – and the following also attended the meeting: * Representing Al-Bisher Legal Group – Mr. Ahmed Fahad Al-Duaij . * Shareholders holding 314,685,130 shares, in person and by proxy, representing 82.503% of the total share capital of the company, which amounts to 157,950,792 shares, after excluding treasury shares. After the announcement of the legal quorum for the validity of the Extraordinary General Assembly meeting, Mr. Ahmed Abdulaziz Ali Al-Mutawa – Chairman of the Board of Directors and Chief Executive Officer – opened the meeting session, welcoming the attendees, and then proceeded to present the items on the Extraordinary General Assembly's agenda for discussion. The following was done: 1. Mr. Chairman of the Board of Directors and CEO reviewed the letter received from the Public Authority for Investment by Mr. Saud Hadhel Salem Al-Khalawi – a request to provide the Public Authority for Investment with the necessary reports regarding the listing of Al-Qurain for Car Trading Company on the Kuwait Stock Exchange for their review and study and to make an appropriate decision thereon. Accordingly, the Assembly agreed to postpone the discussion of items one and two of the attached agenda for two months from this date, provided that another meeting is called to discuss the same two items on April 19, 2025, and make an appropriate decision regarding them. 2. For item three, the Assembly approved the amendment of the text of Article (5) of the Memorandum of Incorporation and the text of Article (4) of the Articles of Association to comply with the requirements of the International Standard Industrial Classification of All Economic Activities (ISIC) . **(Text Before Amendment)** * The purposes for which the company was established are: 1. Importing, exporting, selling, and maintaining cars and their spare parts. 2. Representing car companies and obtaining their agencies. 3. Importing and exporting industrial and heavy machinery and equipment. The company may have an interest in or participate in any way with entities or be affiliated with them. 4. Activity of importing and selling chemical materials, including special car batteries (Text after Amendment) * **Activities to be Reconciled and Added:** Activities to be Reconciled and Added | No. | Activity Code | Activity Name | |:-------|:---|:---| | 1 | 461030 | Export and Import Offices | | 2 | 941112 | Commercial Representation Agencies | | 3 | 461033 | Import of Chemical Materials | | 4 | 453010 | Wholesale of Car Spare Parts | | 5 | 451000 | Sale and Purchase of Motor Vehicles | | 6 | 452000 | Car Repair Garage | | 7 | 465988 | Wholesale of Light and Heavy Equipment | | 8 | 473040 | Retail of Car Spare Parts | | 9 | 475925 | Retail of Light and Heavy Equipment and their Spare Parts | | 10 | 771010 | Rental of Passenger Cars Without Driver | | 11 | 331507 | Repair and Maintenance of Light and Heavy Equipment | | 12 | 451010 | Wholesale and Retail Trade of Motor Vehicles (New and Used) | | 13 | 452010 | Car Mechanical Repair | | 14 | 649917 | Sale and Purchase of Shares and Bonds for the Company's Account | | 15 | 452043 | Car Weighing/Alignment | | 16 | 453020 | Wholesale of Car Tires | | 17 | 473050 | Retail of Car Tires and Accessories | | 18 | 452031 | Car Blacksmith Workshops | | 19 | 451013 | Wholesale and Retail of New Heavy Transport Vehicles | | 20 | 453040 | Car Seat Upholstery and Sale of Car Decorations and Accessories | | 21 | 452070 | Repair and Installation of Car Glass | | 22 | 452051 | Car Washing and Waxing | | 23 | 453030 | Wholesale and Retail of Vehicle and Truck Batteries and their Accessories | | 24 | 465961 | Wholesale of All Types of Mechanical Tools and Equipment of Any Type or Material |
other electronic devices, their spare parts, and the necessary equipment for their operation. 2- Providing all technical services related to computers and other electronic devices, and acting as developers and managers in related businesses. 3- Maintenance of computers and other electronic devices, and establishing maintenance workshops and spare parts stores for these devices. 4- Installation, manufacturing, and assembly of computers and other electronic devices under license from foreign companies, and developing related patent rights, after obtaining the necessary government licenses. 5- Acting as agents for foreign companies dealing with these devices, spare parts, and their complementary and related devices. The company may have an interest in or participate in any way with entities engaged in similar activities or that may assist it in achieving its objectives in Kuwait or abroad, and it may acquire or merge with such entities. 6- Developing computer systems and software, and computer Arabization systems. 7- Developing communication and telecommunication systems and software, and Arabization of mobile phones and portable devices and their accessories. 8- Selling, purchasing, installing, and designing computer systems and software, electronic communication devices, and internal communication networks, marketing them, and providing their operation and maintenance services. 9- Training governmental bodies, institutions, and individuals on integrated systems, e-commerce, and e-government. 10- Building and operating computer communication networks connected to databases. 11- Designing and equipping electronic computer centers and information systems for the benefit of the company and third parties. 12- Marketing and maintenance of electronic safety and security systems, and providing connectivity services, electronic messaging services, and electronic information banks. 13- Manufacturing, producing, and operating smart cards (subject to the approval of the Public Authority for Industry ). 14- Owning the necessary contracting and real estate for carrying out its activities within the limits permitted by law. 15- Utilizing the financial surpluses available to the company by investing them in financial portfolios managed by specialized companies and entities. 16- Opening private training institutes (adding the activity in the license only) after the approval of the Public Authority for Applied Education and Training , and training may not be practiced except after obtaining a special license from the Public Authority for Applied Education and Training and after completing all prescribed and required legal procedures in this regard. Text of Article (4) of the Articles of Association: Text after Amendment: The purposes for which the company was established are: 1- Trading, exporting, importing, leasing, and renting computers and other electronic devices - At the conclusion of the meeting, Mr. Ahmed Abdulaziz Al-Alami Al-Mutawa - Chairman of the Board of Directors and Chief Executive Officer - extended his thanks to the attendees, and the Extraordinary General Assembly meeting concluded at 10:30 AM on the same day. After the quorum for the Extraordinary General Assembly (Adjourned) was met and the legal quorum was announced, he began presenting and discussing the items on the agenda of the Extraordinary General Assembly (Adjourned) as follows:

chunks.title: Current Text:
chunks.text_en: The purposes for which the company was established are: 1- Owning shares in Kuwaiti or foreign joint-stock companies, as well as owning shares or stakes in Kuwaiti or foreign limited liability companies, or participating in the establishment of both types of these companies, managing them, and guaranteeing them to third parties. 2- Lending to companies in which it owns shares and guaranteeing them to third parties. In this case, the holding company's participation in the capital of the borrowing company must not be less than 20% at least. 3- Owning industrial property rights such as patents, trademarks, industrial designs, or any other related rights, and leasing them to other companies for exploitation, whether inside or outside Kuwait. 4- Owning the necessary contracts and real estate for carrying out its activities within the limits permitted by law. 5- Utilizing the financial surpluses available to the company by investing them in financial and real estate portfolios managed by specialized companies. The company may carry out the aforementioned activities in the State of Kuwait and abroad, either directly or by agency. The company may have an interest in or participate in any way with entities that engage in similar activities or that assist it in achieving its objectives in Kuwait or abroad, and it may establish, participate in, acquire, or affiliate with these entities.

chunks.title: Text After Amendment:
chunks.text_en:
The purposes for which the company was established are: 1) Holding company activities – Code No. (642000) 2) Managing its subsidiary companies or participating in the management of other companies in which it holds shares and providing the necessary support to them – Code No. (642010) 3) Investing its funds in trading shares, bonds, and other securities – Code No. (642020) 4) Owning the necessary real estate and contracts for carrying out its work within the limits permitted by law – Code No. (642030) 5) Financing or lending to companies in which it owns shares or stakes and guaranteeing them to third parties. In this case, the holding company's participation in the capital of the borrowing company must not be less than twenty percent – Code No. (642040) 6) Owning intellectual property rights such as patents, trademarks, industrial designs, franchise rights, and other intangible rights, and exploiting and leasing them to its subsidiary holding companies and others – Code No. (642050) 7) Owning shares for the company's account only – Code No. (642051) 8) Office for managing holding activities – Code No. (642070) The company may carry out the aforementioned activities in the State of Kuwait and abroad, either directly or by agency. The company may have an interest in or participate in any way with entities that engage in similar activities or that assist it in achieving its objectives in Kuwait or abroad, and it may establish, participate in, acquire, or affiliate with these entities. 2.38% of the total attending shares abstained from voting on this item. At the conclusion of the meeting, Mr. Rashid Yaqoub Al-Nafisi – Chairman of the Board of Directors and Chairman of the Extraordinary General Assembly (Adjourned) – extended his thanks to the attendees. The proceedings of the Extraordinary General Assembly (Adjourned) meeting concluded at 11:45 AM on the same day.
