Discussion and approval of the amendment of the Company's Articles of Association in accordance with Law No. (1) of (2016), after obtaining the approval of both the Ministry of Commerce and Industry and the Central Bank of Kuwait , as follows: Amendment of Article (28) of the Articles of Association Article (28) Before Amendment: Article (28) "The Board of Directors may distribute work among its members according to the nature of the company's business. The Board may also delegate one of its members, a committee from among its members, or a third party to perform one or more specific tasks, supervise an aspect of the company's activity, or exercise some of the powers or competencies entrusted to the Board." Article (28) After Amendment: Article (28) "The Board of Directors shall have the broadest powers in managing the company and shall carry out all actions required for the company's management in accordance with its purposes. This authority shall only be limited by what is stipulated in the law, regulations, the company's articles of association, or the resolutions of the General Assembly. The Board of Directors may lend, mortgage the company's real estate, conclude guarantees, releases, arbitration, and conciliation. Amendment of Article (28) of the Articles of Association Article (28) Before Amendment: Article (28) "The Board of Directors may distribute work among its members according to the nature of the company's business. The Board may also delegate one of its members, a committee from among its members, or a third party to perform one or more specific tasks, supervise an aspect of the company's activity, or exercise some of the powers or competencies entrusted to the Board." Article (28) After Amendment: Article (28) "The Board of Directors shall have the broadest powers in managing the company and shall carry out all actions required for the company's management in accordance with its purposes. This authority shall only be limited by what is stipulated in the law, regulations, the company's articles of association, or the resolutions of the General Assembly. The Board may also delegate one of its members, a committee from among its members, or a third party to perform one or more specific tasks, supervise an aspect of the company's activity, or exercise some of the powers or competencies entrusted to the Board." Text Before Amendment:
The company shall be managed by a Board of Directors composed of (5) members, of whom the entity or entities entitled to appoint their representatives to the Board of Directors shall appoint members in proportion to their ownership in the company's capital, and the remaining members shall be elected by secret ballot. | Article No. (13) of the Articles of Association
Text After Amendment:
The company shall be managed by a Board of Directors composed of (3) members. | | Article No. (21) of the Articles of Association
Text Before Amendment:
1- The Board of Directors must meet at least six times during one fiscal year. The meeting shall be convened upon an invitation from the Chairman of the Board by any means, including modern communication methods.
2- A Board of Directors meeting shall not be valid unless at least half of the members are present. Attendance by proxy is not permitted at Board meetings. Meetings may be held using modern communication methods, and resolutions may be passed by circulation with the approval of all Board members. | Article No. (21) of the Articles of Association
Text After Amendment:
1- The Board of Directors must meet at least six times during one fiscal year. The meeting shall be convened upon an invitation from the Chairman of the Board by any means, including modern communication methods.
2- A Board of Directors meeting shall not be valid unless at least half of the members are present. Attendance by proxy is not permitted at Board meetings. Meetings may be held using modern communication methods, and resolutions may be passed by circulation with the approval of all Board members. |
At the conclusion of the meeting, Mr. / Nawaf Faisal Al-Hammadi – Chairman of the Board of Directors (Session Chairman) thanked the attendees, and the Extraordinary General Assembly meeting concluded at 11:30 AM on the same day. chunks.title: Announcement
chunks.text_en:
The Ministry of Interior announces Practice No. (L.A. 2024/176-2025) for the supply, installation, operation, and maintenance of security systems for the General Department of Information for the General Department of Security Systems at the Ministry of Interior . Therefore, specialized companies/establishments in this field should refer to the General Department of Financial Affairs – Tenders Department (in Al-Ardiya Industrial Area ) to obtain the documents for this practice for a fee of only (150) K.D. (one hundred and fifty Kuwaiti Dinars) non-refundable (via K-Net ). An initial guarantee of (3500) K.D. for a period of (90) days must be submitted (to be attached with the bid submitted in the practice), noting that the final insurance for the practice is (10%) of the total contract value. They must bring a copy of the practice announcement from the Official Gazette (Kuwait Today / Al-Kuwait Al-Youm ) or the Ministry's website, and the following documents: - Valid registration receipt with the Ministry of Interior . Or - A registration request with the Ministry, followed by purchasing the practice documents. Please note that the closing date (submission of bids) is Wednesday, 18/6/2025, from 8:00 AM until 1:00 PM. No bids submitted before or after the mentioned time will be accepted. Bids must be submitted to the Bid Opening Committee – Al-Siddiq School – First Floor. Two mandatory preliminary meetings will be held to answer inquiries from companies participating in the practice: the first on Wednesday, 28/4/2025, and the second on Wednesday, 30/4/2025. chunks.title: Minutes of the Meeting of the Births and Deaths Committee <orig>لجنة المواليد والوفيات</orig>
chunks.text_en: Held on 23/3/2025, Session No. 10 After reviewing the submitted requests for amending data in the birth and death registers, it was decided to proceed with amending the birth and death registers. Birth and death certificates will be issued after fifteen days from the date of publication of this announcement. The amendment will be made in the registers, and birth or death certificates will be issued according to the following amendment: The Births and Deaths Committee , stipulated in Article 15 of Law No. 36/1969 and formed by Ministerial Resolution No. 1650/2021 , met. * Dr. / Head of the General Medical Council - Chairman * Mr. / Head of Registration Department - Member * Mr. / Legal Researcher - Member Minutes of the Meeting of the Births and Deaths Committee Held on 23/3/2025, Committee Session No. 10 Ms. Alia Al-Saffar acted as the Committee's Secretary. The Committee reviewed requests from No. (((128))) to No. (((142))) in the attached list concerning data amendments in birth and death registers for dropped birth and death registration requests. The legal period (fifteen days) from the date of the last announcement has expired, and no objection has been submitted to the authorities concerned with the registers. Birth and death certificates will be issued. * Head of Research and Opinion Department * Head of Deaths Department * Head of Records Department * Legal Researcher * Committee Secretary * Head of the General Medical Council * Director of the Central Birth Register Department <table_caption>Name Changes Approved Under Law No. <header> 76 Sunday, 22 Shawwal 1446 AH - 20/4/2025 AD

chunks.title: Announcement of Real Estate Public Auction
chunks.text_en: The Clerk's Office at the General Court announces the public auction of the property described below, on Thursday, 15/5/2025, in Hall 52, Second Floor, Palace of Justice , at 9:00 AM, in execution of the court judgment issued in lawsuit No. 2022/6 Sales/3 . Filed by: Aziz Haidar Hassan Al-Hamr . Against: 1- Haitham Abbas Yaqoub Al-Aryan . 2- Kuwait Credit Bank .

chunks.title: First: Property Description: According to the attached Description Certificate
chunks.text_en: - Property Document No. 2017/6231 in Al-Khiran area - Plot 378 - Block 7 - from Plan No. 35404 - with an area of 600 m2. * Note: - There are major violations on the roof with an area of 2m2, violation report No. 12697 dated 2/5/2024. * Inspection: The property subject of the lawsuit is located at the site of the dispute in the new Al-Khiran residential area, Block (7) - Street (720) - House (12) Plot (378), Automated Number: 21124504, with Document No. 2017/6231, with an area of 600m2. The inspection was conducted in the presence and under the guidance of the defendant and Mr. Sa'ad Al-Mutairi , the knowledge expert sought by the administration, while the plaintiff failed to attend. The inspection was carried out as follows: - The property subject of the lawsuit is a newly constructed private residence located on one street and an alleyway, consisting of three floors with Sigma facades, central air conditioning, and an elevator. - The ground floor consists of: a diwaniya with its bathroom, a hall, 4 bedrooms, a kitchen, and a maid's room with its bathroom, and it is independent. - The first floor consists of: a hall, 6 bedrooms, 5 bathrooms, and a kitchen, and it is not independent. - The second floor consists of two apartments (Apartment No. 1 consists of a hall, a kitchen, 3 rooms, and 3 bathrooms) and (Apartment No. 2 consists of a hall, a kitchen, 4 rooms, and 4 bathrooms), and it is not independent. - Mr. Sa'ad Al-Mutairi , the knowledge expert , took all his observations in preparation for estimating the property's value.

chunks.title: Second: Auction Terms:
chunks.text_en: 1. First: The auction shall start with a base price of 210,600 K.D. (Two Hundred Ten Thousand Six Hundred Kuwaiti Dinars ). Participation in the auction requires payment of at least one-fifth of that price by a certified check from the drawee bank or by a letter of guarantee from one of the banks in favor of the Enforcement Department at the Ministry of Justice . 2. Second: The bidder whose offer is approved by the judge must deposit the full approved price, expenses, and registration fees immediately upon the sale session. 3. Third: If the bidder whose offer was approved does not deposit the full price, they must deposit at least one-fifth of the price; otherwise, the bidding will be resumed at their expense in the same session based on the price at which the sale was previously awarded. 4. Fourth: If the bidder deposits the price in the subsequent session, the auction shall be awarded to them, unless someone comes forward in that session willing to purchase with a ten percent increase, accompanied by the deposit of the full auction price. In this case, the bidding shall be resumed in the same session based on this new price. 5. Fifth: If the initial bidder fails to deposit the full price in the subsequent session and no one comes forward with a ten percent increase, the bidding shall immediately be resumed at their expense based on the price at which it was awarded to them in the previous session. In this session, no offer not accompanied by the deposit of its full value shall be considered. The defaulting bidder shall be liable for any decrease in the property's price. 6. Sixth: In all cases, the successful bidder shall bear the fees for transfer and registration of ownership, enforcement procedure expenses amounting to 200 K.D., legal and expert fees, and advertising and publication costs for the sale in daily newspapers. 7. Seventh: This announcement is published in application of the law, and those directly involved in the sale procedures do so at their own responsibility, without the Clerk's Office at the General Court bearing any liability. 8. Eighth: The successful bidder acknowledges that they have inspected the property in a manner that negates any ignorance of its condition. Notice: 1- This sale announcement shall be published in the Official Gazette in accordance with Article 266 of the Code of Civil Procedure . 2- The auction award judgment is subject to appeal within seven days from the date of its pronouncement, in accordance with Article 277 of the Code of Civil Procedure . 3- The last paragraph of Article 276 of the Code of Civil Procedure states that "If the expropriated owner is residing in the property, they shall remain therein as a tenant by operation of law, and the successful bidder shall be obliged to draw up a lease contract in their favor at a fair market rent." President of the General Court Fifth: If the first bidder fails to deposit the full price in the subsequent session and no one comes forward to bid with a ten percent increase , the auction shall be immediately re-held based on the price at which it was awarded in the previous session. No bid in this session shall be considered unless accompanied by a full deposit of its value. The defaulting bidder shall be liable for any shortfall in the property's price. Sixth: In all cases, the successful bidder shall bear the property transfer and registration fees , enforcement procedure expenses amounting to 200 K.D. , legal and expert fees , and advertising and publication costs for the sale in daily newspapers. Seventh: This announcement is published in application of the Law of Claimants for Sale Procedures and at their responsibility, without the Clerk's Office at the General Court bearing any liability. Eighth: The successful bidder acknowledges that they have inspected the property with an inspection that negates ignorance. Notice: 1- This sale announcement shall be published in the Official Gazette in accordance with Article 266 of the Code of Civil Procedure . 2- The auction award judgment is appealable within seven days from the date of its pronouncement, in accordance with Article 277 of the Code of Civil Procedure . 3- The last paragraph of Article 276 of the Code of Civil Procedure states that "If the person whose ownership was dispossessed is residing in the property, they shall remain in it as a tenant by force of law, and the successful bidder shall be obligated to draw up a lease contract in their favor at the market rent ." President of the General Court Announcement of Property Sale by Public Auction The Clerk's Office at the General Court announces the public auction of the property described below, on Thursday, 25/5/2025, in Hall 52, on the second floor of the Palace of Justice , at 9:00 AM, in execution of the court judgment issued in Case No. 2023/28 Sales/3 . Filed by: Suha Jabr Ali Al-Jabr . Against: 1- Adila Jabr Ali Al-Jabr . 2- Karima Jabr Ali Al-Jabr . 3- Abeer Jabr Ali Al-Jabr . 4- Nada Jabr Ali Al-Jabr . 5- Nawaf Jabr Ali Al-Jabr . 6- Fahad Jabr Ali Al-Jabr . 7- Director of the Enforcement Department – in his capacity. First: Property Description : According to the attached description certificate . Property Document No. 1972/972 located in Al-Funaitis area – Block No. 9 – Plot 1 – Plan M/19846 – with an area of 2481 m2. *Inspection : On 30/4/2019, we proceeded to inspect the property subject of the case, accompanied by Mr. Salman Hamad Al-Bahwa , the knowledgeable expert , in the personal presence of the second defendant. The plaintiff and the remaining defendants failed to attend. The inspection was carried out as follows: – The property subject of the case, located in Al-Funaitis area – Block 9 – Street 46 – Plot No. 22 , was visited. It consists of a building comprising a ground floor + half first floor + roof. The building includes a courtyard with parking spaces in addition to a garden . All these buildings are surrounded by a fence with two gates , one overlooking a main street and the other a side street . The disputed property is situated on two streets (front and back) and is bordered by a dirt lot to the east and a neighbor to the west, which is a chain of restaurants . The total area of the disputed property is 2481 m2. - The buildings of the disputed property have a stone facade + Sigma paint , are in old condition, and have aluminum doors and windows . - The first building consists of a reception hall + dining room + kitchen + 4 bedrooms + 5 bathrooms . The floors are marble , the walls are painted at the Ministry of Justice . Second: The bidder whose offer is approved by the judge must deposit the full approved price, expenses, and registration fees upon the conclusion of the sale session. Third: If the bidder whose offer was approved fails to deposit the full price, they must deposit at least one-fifth of the price, and the bidding shall be resumed at their expense in the same session based on the price at which the sale was previously awarded. Fourth: If the bidder deposits the price in the subsequent session, the auction shall be awarded to them, unless someone comes forward in that session willing to purchase with a ten percent increase, accompanied by the deposit of the full auction price. In this case, the bidding shall be resumed in the same session based on this new price. Fifth: If the initial bidder fails to deposit the full price in the subsequent session, and no one comes forward with a ten percent increase, the bidding shall immediately be resumed at their expense based on the price at which it was awarded to them in the previous session. In this session, no offer not accompanied by a full deposit of its value shall be considered. The defaulting bidder shall be liable for any shortfall in the property's price. Sixth: In all cases, the successful bidder shall bear the transfer and property registration fees, enforcement procedure expenses amounting to 200 K.D. , legal and expert fees, and advertising and publication costs for the sale in daily newspapers. Seventh: This announcement is published in accordance with the law, and bids are invited for the sale procedures at the bidders' responsibility, without the Clerk's Office at the General Court bearing any liability. Eighth: The successful bidder acknowledges that they have inspected the property in a manner that negates any ignorance of its condition. Notice: 1- This sale announcement is published in the Official Gazette in accordance with Article 266 of the Code of Civil Procedure 14. 2- The auction award is subject to appeal within seven days from the date of judgment pronouncement, in accordance with Article 277 of the Code of Civil Procedure . 3- The last paragraph of Article 276 of the Code of Civil Procedure states that "If the dispossessed owner is residing in the property, they shall remain therein as a tenant by force of law, and the successful bidder shall be obligated to draw up a lease contract in their favor at a fair market rent ." President of the General Court Announcement of Public Auction for Property Sale The Clerk's Office at the General Court announces the public auction of the property described below, on Thursday, 15/5/2025, in Hall 52, on the second floor of the Palace of Justice , at 9:00 AM, in execution of the court judgment issued in lawsuits No. 166 and 175/2023 (Sales 3), Filed by the heirs of the late / Abdullah Ibrahim Al-Shaheen Al-Ali (namely): First: Ibrahim Abdullah Mohammed Al-Shaheen Al-Ali . 1- Shaheen Abdullah Ibrahim Al-Shaheen Al-Ali 2- Mohammed Abdullah Ibrahim Al-Shaheen Al-Ali 3- Saqr Abdullah Ibrahim Al-Shaheen Al-Ali 4- Noura Abdullah Ibrahim Al-Shaheen Al-Ali 5- Munira Abdullah Ibrahim Al-Shaheen Al-Ali Second: Munira Ali Mubarak Al-Shatti . First: Property Description: According to the attached description certificate. Property Document No. 2814/2008, located in Hawally - Plot No. 115 - Parcel 6 - Map M / 20012 - described as an investment building with an area of 763 m². Note: There are violations. 1- Dividing the basement into offices and leasing them. 2- Creating 4 apartments in the mezzanine. 3- Incorporating apartments into the distributor on all floors. 4- Building a guard room on the ground floor. 5- Demolishing the glass and light material suspended structures. 6- Placing light material rooms on the ground floor. *Inspection: During the expert session dated 23/7/2024, we moved to the property subject of the lawsuit, located in Hawally area - Plot (11) - Qutaiba Bin Muslim Street ) Parcel (11506), in the presence and guidance of the plaintiffs' agent and in the presence of the administration's knowledge expert, Mr. Salman Al-Bahwa . The first defendant failed to appear. The inspection was conducted as follows: 1- The property subject of the lawsuit conforms to the general specifications of Document No. (2814) issued by the Real Estate Registration and Documentation Department at the Ministry of Justice , and its area is (763) square meters. 2- The property is an investment building overlooking a main street from the front, bordered by neighbors on the remaining sides. 3- The property subject of the lawsuit consists of a ground floor, (9) floors with (4) apartments per floor, and a full basement with two apartments. 4- The property has (2) elevators and (2) staircases leading to the floors. 5- The representative of the plaintiffs stated that the property is leased and generates monthly income. 6- The property has numerous unshaded parking spaces. 7- The cladding of the property subject of the lawsuit is limestone (National Industries ). 8- The property's air conditioning is central. The administration's knowledge expert took the necessary notes in preparation for estimating its monthly income value. Secondly: Auction Conditions First: The auction shall commence at a basic price of 1,548,000 K.D. "One Million Five Hundred Forty-Eight Thousand Kuwaiti Dinars ". Participation in the auction requires payment of at least one-fifth of that price by a certified check from the drawee bank or by a letter of guarantee from one of the banks in favor of the Enforcement Department at the Ministry of Justice . Second: The person whose bid is approved by the judge must deposit the full approved price, expenses, and registration fees at the time of the sale session. Third: If the person whose bid is approved does not deposit the full price, they must deposit at least one-fifth of the price, otherwise the bidding will be reopened at their expense in the same session based on the price at which the sale was previously awarded. Fourth: If the bidder deposits the price in the subsequent session, the auction will be awarded to them, unless someone comes forward in that session willing to purchase with a ten percent increase, accompanied by the deposit of the full auction price. In this case, the bidding will be reopened in the same session based on this price. Fifth: If the first bidder fails to deposit the full price in the subsequent session and no one comes forward with a ten percent increase, the bidding will immediately be reopened at their expense based on the price at which it was awarded to them in the previous session. In this session, no bid not accompanied by the deposit of its full value will be considered. The defaulting bidder shall be liable for any shortfall in the property's price. Sixth: In all cases, the successful bidder shall bear the transfer and property registration fees, execution procedure expenses amounting to 200 K.D. , legal and expert fees, and advertising and publication costs for the sale in daily newspapers. Seventh: This announcement is published in accordance with the law and at the request of those initiating the sale procedures, and at their responsibility, without the Department of Clerks at the General Court bearing any responsibility. Eighth: The successful bidder acknowledges that they have inspected the property in a manner that negates any ignorance. Notice: 1- This sale announcement shall be published in the Official Gazette in accordance with Article 266 of the Code of Civil Procedure . 2- The auction award judgment is subject to appeal within seven days from the date of its pronouncement, in accordance with Article 277 of the Code of Civil Procedure . 3- The last paragraph of Article 276 of the Code of Civil Procedure states that "If the person whose ownership has been divested is residing in the property, they shall remain therein as a tenant by operation of law, and the successful bidder shall be obligated to draw up a lease contract in their favor accordingly." President of the General Court Property Description: As stated in the Municipality's property description certificate. An undivided share of 50% in property No. 2014/3931 located in Saad Al-Abdullah area – Plot (755), Block No. (8) in plan 38797 – with a total area of 400m2, to be sold by public auction with a base price of 155,000 K.D. (Six Million, One Hundred Fifty-Six Thousand Kuwaiti Dinars ) 2- Abdul Rahman Saleh Khalid Al-Jasser . 3- Ibrahim Saleh Khalid Al-Jasser . 4- Abdullah Saleh Khalid Al-Jasser . First: Property Description: As stated in the Municipality's Property Description Certificate. - Property Document No. 1978/7665 located in Al-Ardiya area – Plot No. 88 – Block No. 1 – from Plan No. M/29687 with an area of 750m2 , to be sold by public auction at a base price of 648,000 K.D. (Six Hundred and Forty-Eight Thousand Kuwaiti Dinars ). ** According to the description certificate: The property is a private residence consisting of (basement + ground floor + first floor + second floor + roof) and has a violation, which is a light-mode construction on the ground floor roof – Property Number: 522600021415 ** According to the expert's report: - The disputed property located in Al-Ardiya area – Block 1 – Street 2 – House 27 1- The disputed property is a relatively old house with an area of 750 square meters , consisting of 3 floors, with a renovated building at the back, which is a diwaniya (reception hall) with a ground floor and a basement, featuring exterior finishing and beige stone. The property is 'belly and back' (front and back access), located on a street from one side and a large rear setback approximately 25 meters long. 2- The expert examination did not reveal the presence of tenants in the property. 3- The main building consists of 3 floors. The ground floor comprises two halls, one room, two bathrooms, and a kitchen with ceramic flooring, and central air conditioning that is not working, along with units (and according to the statement of the first defendant, the ground floor is occupied by the defendants' mother and their divorced sister). The first floor contains four rooms, two bathrooms, a hall, and a kitchen with ceramic and carpet flooring, and central air conditioning along with units (and the expert examination did not reveal that the floor is occupied). The expert examination was not enabled to inspect the second floor (due to the absence of the plaintiffs or one of them), and the expert examination did not reveal anyone occupying it, according to the statement of the first defendant, who stated that the floor contains two bathrooms, 4 rooms, a hall, and a kitchen, central air conditioning that is not working, along with units, and carpet and ceramic flooring. 4- The rear part of the property, overlooking the setback, consists of a diwaniya and a ground floor bathroom. * Inspection: * The expert conducted an inspection session on 14/4/2024 by moving to inspect the property subject of the lawsuit, located in Fintas area, Block (5) , Sabah Al-Salem Street, Plot No. (15) , and according to the Civil ID program (Kuwait Finder), it bears plot number (800000) – Automated Number 90884865 . This was in the presence of Mr. Adel Al-Aslawi , the circuit expert, and under the guidance of the plaintiff bank's agent. Both the first defendant and the second defendant, or their legal representatives, failed to attend despite being duly notified. The inspection was carried out as follows: 1. The property is a large, fenced vacant land from the outside, directly overlooking the sea with a 61-meter linear frontage + a rear access separating it from the Travel Hotel + a side yard. 2. The total area of the property is (4762m2) according to property document No. 2009/4841 attached to the lawsuit file. 3. During the inspection, it was found that the main doors were closed, and a wooden side door was open. It was also found that the plot's land had been excavated previously and the walls reinforced, with water flooding the plot's land and construction work having been halted for some time. Second: Auction Terms: First: The auction shall commence at the property's base price. Participation in the auction requires payment of at least one-fifth of that price by a certified check from the drawee bank or by a letter of guarantee from one of the banks in favor of the Enforcement Department at the Ministry of Justice . Second: The bidder whose offer is approved by the judge must deposit the full approved price, expenses, and registration fees at the time of the sale session. Third: If the bidder whose offer was approved does not deposit the full price, they must deposit at least one-fifth of the price; otherwise, the bidding will be re-opened at their expense in the same session based on the price at which the sale was initially awarded. Fourth: If the bidder fails to deposit the price in the subsequent session, the auction shall be awarded to them, unless someone comes forward in that session willing to purchase with a ten percent increase, accompanied by the deposit of the full price. In this case, the bidding shall be re-opened in the same session based on the new price. Fifth: If the first bidder fails to deposit the full price in the subsequent session and no one comes forward to bid with a ten percent increase, the bidding shall immediately be re-opened at their expense based on the price at which it was awarded to them in the previous session. In this session, no offer not accompanied by the deposit of its full value shall be considered. The defaulting bidder shall be liable for any decrease in the property's price. Sixth: In all cases, the successful bidder shall bear the transfer and property registration fees, enforcement procedure expenses amounting to 200 K.D. , legal and expert fees, and advertising and publication expenses for the sale in daily newspapers. Seventh: This announcement shall be published in application of the law, and those directly involved in the sale procedures shall be responsible for them, without the Court Clerk's Office bearing any responsibility. Eighth: The successful bidder acknowledges that they have inspected the property with an inspection that negates ignorance (i.e., a thorough inspection). Secondly: Auction Terms: Firstly: The auction shall commence with a base price of 245,000 Kuwaiti Dinars (Two Hundred Forty-Five Thousand Kuwaiti Dinars ). Participation in the auction requires payment of at least one-fifth of that price by a certified check from the drawee bank or by a letter of guarantee from one of the banks in favor of the Enforcement Department at the Ministry of Justice . Secondly: The bidder whose offer is approved by the judge must fully deposit the approved price, expenses, and registration fees. Thirdly: If the bidder whose offer was approved fails to deposit the full price, they must deposit at least one-fifth of the price; otherwise, the bidding shall be re-opened at their expense in the same session based on the price at which the sale was previously awarded. Fourthly: If the bidder deposits the price in the subsequent session, the auction shall be awarded to them, unless someone comes forward in that session willing to purchase with a ten percent increase, accompanied by a deposit of the full auction price. In this case, the bidding shall be re-opened in the same session based on this new price. Fifthly: If the initial bidder fails to deposit the full price in the subsequent session, and no one comes forward to bid with the ten percent increase, the bidding shall immediately be re-opened at their expense based on the price at which it was awarded to them in the previous session. No offer in this session shall be considered valid unless accompanied by a deposit of its full value. The defaulting bidder shall be liable for any shortfall in the property's price. Sixthly: In all cases, the successful bidder shall bear the transfer and property registration fees, enforcement procedure expenses amounting to 200 Kuwaiti Dinars , legal and expert fees, and advertising and publication costs for the sale in daily newspapers. Seventhly: This announcement is published in application of the law, and those directly involved in the sale procedures are responsible for them, without the Clerk's Office at the General Court bearing any responsibility. Eighthly: The successful bidder acknowledges that they have inspected the property in a manner that negates any ignorance of its condition. Notice: 1- This sale announcement shall be published in the Official Gazette in accordance with Article 266 of the Code of Civil Procedure . 2- The auction award judgment is subject to appeal within seven days from the date of its pronouncement, in accordance with Article 277 of the Code of Civil Procedure . 3- The last paragraph of Article 276 of the Code of Civil Procedure states that "If the expropriated owner is residing in the property, they shall remain therein as a tenant by operation of law, and the bidder shall be obligated to draw up a lease contract in their favor at a fair market rent." In this case, the bidding shall be resumed in the same session based on this new price. **Fifth:** If the first bidder fails to deposit the full price in the subsequent session and no one comes forward with a ten percent increase, the bidding shall immediately resume at their expense based on the price at which it was awarded to them in the previous session. No bid in this session shall be considered valid unless accompanied by a deposit of its full value. chunks.title: Inspection:
chunks.text_en: On 7/2/2022, we moved to the disputed property located in Salwa area – Block (2) – Street (3) – Building (19) – Plot (77) – Automated Address Number (11834473), to inspect the property on site in preparation for its valuation, in the presence of Mr. Abdullah Bashir Abeer Salman (the plaintiff personally) and their agent for plaintiffs from the first to the sixth, and Ms. Mariam Farhan Sulaiman Bughaith (the defendant personally), and with the assistance of Mr. Knowledge Expert / Khalid Al-Khader from the Knowledge Experts Department at the General Administration of Experts . The inspection was carried out as follows: The disputed property conforms in terms of general and specific specifications to Document No. (1993/7227) and has an area of (832 m²). The disputed property overlooks one street and has neighbors on three sides. It is divided into two parts, each with an area of (416 m²), sharing one fence but differing in design, with a barrier between them. Their front facade has a setback of approximately 2.5 m. - The disputed property consists of two parts, detailed as follows: 1- Specifications of the First Part of the Disputed Property (B19): - Consists of two and a half floors. The building condition of the first part (B19) is good and recently renovated. - The ground floor consists of a diwaniya with its bathroom and washbasins + a driver's room with its bathroom + 3 halls + a bedroom + a large kitchen + a bathroom + a courtyard for car entrance. - The first floor consists of 3 master bedrooms + 2 + a bedroom + a bathroom + a small hall. - The rooftop consists of: a maid's room + a laundry room + a bathroom + a storage room. - Air conditioning in the first part: units. - There is an elevator in the first part. The flooring is ceramic, and the walls are painted. There is one internal staircase and 2 new external staircases leading to the rooftop. - The exterior facade of the first part: Sigma (paint type) in beige color. - There are two entrances to the first part, and all windows have shutters. 2- Specifications of the Second Part of the Disputed Property (19): Consists of two and a quarter floors. The house condition is old. - The ground floor consists of: a courtyard and car garage + 2 halls + a living room + a dining room + a preparatory kitchen + 2 bathrooms + a small storage room + a maid's room + a main kitchen (in the courtyard). - The first floor consists of: a hall + a master bedroom with a dressing room + 2 bedrooms + a bathroom + a preparatory kitchen. - The rooftop consists of: a laundry room + a bathroom. There are two entrances. - Staircases: One internal staircase serving the first floor and the rooftop. - Flooring: Sicilia marble and parquet on the first floor. - Walls: painted. - Air conditioning: central. - Elevators: None. - Exterior finishes: Jordanian stone in pink color, with the facade studded with brown ceramic. Mr. Knowledge Expert inspected the property in preparation for valuing the disputed property and submitting a report for its sale in the public auction.

chunks.text_en: The General Court issued, in its session held on 2021-03-18, in Case No.: 001195 / 20 - Commercial Affairs Prosecution Cheque Misdemeanors Report No.: (0000345 / 20 – Shuwaikh Industrial ) Automated Case No.: 201439260 Against the Defendant: Mahmoud Ibrahim Abdullah Al-Sherbini Nationality: Egypt Civil ID No.: 282051509243 an absentia judgment ruling: imprisoning the defendant for six months with labor and enforcement for the charges attributed to him, and ordered his deportation from the country after the execution of the decreed penalty, and ordered the referral of the civil claim to the competent Civil Court to determine its jurisdiction and notify the litigants. The Public Prosecution hereby notifies the convicted person of this judgment and instructs all security authorities to enforce it in accordance with the text of Article 188 of the Code of Criminal Procedures and Trials , noting that The Public Prosecution announces the judgment against him with this ruling and instructs all security agencies to enforce it in accordance with Article 188 of the Code of Criminal Procedures and Trials , noting that if a period of thirty-seven days, calculated from the second day of publication, elapses without the convicted person appealing by objection or appeal, the judgment becomes final and enforceable against him. chunks.title: Correction <orig>استدراك</orig>
chunks.text_en: A material error occurred on the part of the requesting party when publishing the attached announcement below, and therefore it is being published again after correcting the error. Further to our previous letter No. (61) dated 23/12/2024, concerning the publication of decisions issued by the Committee for Lineage Claims and Name Correction at session No. (61/2024) held on 2/12/2024. We inform you that an error occurred in request No. (2117/2024) regarding the correction of the applicant's name to become / Hussein Dhafer Hussein Mohammed Ibrahim Al-Sanea , who holds Civil ID No. (289043000297), and also the correction of his wife's name to become / Badriya Sabah Mohammed Taher Hussein Al-Shirazi , who holds Civil ID No. Whereas the correct applicant is No. (2024/2117), correction of the student's name to become Hussein Dhafer Hussein Ibrahim Al-Sanea , who holds Civil ID No. (289043000297), and also correction of his wife's name to become Badriya Sabah Mohammed Taher Mohammed Hassan Al-Shirazi , who holds Civil ID No. (293072700411), in all their official documents, based on their Kuwaiti nationality certificates. Head of the Committee for Lineage Claims and Name Correction Announcement The General Department of Execution at the Ministry of Justice announces the names of deceased persons for whom funds are deposited with the Department, whose names are listed below.

chunks.title: Ministry of Finance <orig>وزارة المالية</orig>
chunks.text_en:
Announcement Tender (Auction) No. (2024-2023/4) Regarding the license for usufruct, management, and operation of shop No. (17) for the purpose of selling Italian food in Building (10), First Floor - Ministries Complex Tender Details for Italian Food Shop Lease | Tender Date | Closing Date | Price | Guarantee | |:------|:--------|:------|:--------| | 20/4/2025 | 19/5/2015 | 75 K.D. / - | (2%) Initial Deposit for 90 days |
* **Preliminary Meeting**: The preliminary meeting is scheduled for 10:00 AM on 29/4/2025 in Al-Youm Hall - Building No. (17), First Floor. * **Technical Meeting for Opening Bids**: The meeting is on 19/5/2025 at 1:00 PM in the Meeting Room - Financial Affairs Department . The Ministry of Finance announces the tender (auction) No. (2024-2023/4) regarding the license for usufruct, management, and operation of shop No. (17) for the purpose of selling Italian food in Building (10), First Floor - Ministries Complex , according to the terms and specifications contained in the tender documents. These can be obtained from the Claims and Collection Department (Cashier) at the Ministry, located in the Ministries Complex , Ground Floor, Building (11), during official working hours. Payment is made via K-Net device . Bids must include the initial deposit, whether in the form of letters of guarantee or certified bank checks, and must include the name of the submitting company or establishment and the beneficiary, the Ministry of Finance . A National Labor Support Certificate must be attached to the bids, otherwise the bid will be considered void. Note: Follow the official Ministry of Finance website https://www.mof.gov.kw for any updates regarding Auction (2024/23-4) concerning the license to utilize and operate shop No. (17) for the purpose of selling Italian food in Building (10), First Floor – Ministries Complex . Undersecretary of the Ministry of Finance

chunks.title: Auction No. (2461)
chunks.text_en: The Ministry of Finance announces the sale of devices - computers - iPads - printers - computer screens - imaging devices - keyboards and other miscellaneous items no longer in use by the Kuwait Fire Force through a sealed bid auction according to the following details: 1- Location for receiving the terms and conditions booklet and participation offer: Kuwait Fire Force – West Mishref – Main Road – Second Floor – Financial Affairs Department – Revenue Section – Room No. (227), from 20/4/2025 – 24/4/2025. Documents are handed over for a non-refundable fee of (30) Kuwaiti Dinars payable at the Kuwait Fire Force fund. The bidder will sign a declaration and undertaking upon receiving the terms and conditions booklet to adhere to the specified deadline for submitting the bid to the Central Sales Committee at the Ministry of Finance . 2- Inspection Date: (5) working days starting from 20/4/2025 – 24/4/2025. Inspection Time: During official working hours. 3- Location for those wishing to inspect: Al-Rai Workshops Complex – Dirt Yard Warehouse. 4- Date and Place for Submitting Bids and Opening Envelopes: * Bid submission on Sunday, 27/4/2025, at the Ministry of Finance – Ministries Complex – Block 12 – Third Floor – General Storage Affairs Department – Central Sales Section , according to the appointment set for the bidder by the Kuwait Fire Force (no bid will be accepted after the bidder's specified deadline). * Envelope opening and auction holding on Monday, 28/4/2025, at the Ministry of Finance – Ministries Complex – Block 12 – Third Floor – General Storage Affairs Department. * Announcement of the successful bidder on the Ministry of Finance website on Monday, 28/4/2025. * Return of auction checks for unsuccessful bidders on Tuesday, 29/4/2025. * Certified checks / bank guarantees must be submitted in the name of the Kuwait Fire Force . Special Auction Conditions: * The bidder must be a Kuwaiti institution or company registered in the Commercial Register and with the Kuwait Chamber of Commerce and Industry . Foreign entities may participate provided they have a Kuwaiti partner or commercial agent with a notarized contract. (Must attach: copy of Chamber of Commerce and Industry certificate, copy of company license, letter of authorization from the Chamber of Commerce and Industry, all valid). * To receive the auction terms and conditions booklet: Please contact Lieutenant Colonel / Saad Al-Omair , Tel: 95005050 * Auction inspection site official: Engineer / Mohammed Abdeen , Tel: 95590092 * Rapporteur of the Central Sales Committee at the Ministry of Finance / Tel: (22481979) * Website address: www.mof.gov.kw

This is to be done in sealed envelopes according to the terms and specifications contained in the practice documents, which can be obtained from the Services and Procurement Department - Tenders and Practices Section, Ground Floor, Office No. (5) at the Authority's headquarters in Al-Riqqa area during official working hours or through the Public Authority for Sport / Al-Hay'a Al-Amma lil-Riyadha website (Pas.gov.kw/tenders). No inquiries will be considered after the deadline. * All documents are to be submitted on (CD) and a non-copyable hard copy, except for the bid format and the initial eighth , which are to be submitted as a hard copy. Any bid not accompanied by the original or a copy of the receipt of purchase of practice documents, the initial guarantee, and the national labor support certificate issued by the Public Authority for Manpower / Al-Hay'a Al-Amma lil-Quwa Al-Amila for the year 2025 will be excluded. * The bidder must attach a valid "to whom it may concern" certificate issued by the Public Authority for Manpower / Al-Hay'a Al-Amma lil-Quwa Al-Amila stating that there is no suspension on the practitioner's file regarding government contracts registered with the Authority or any of the mentioned violations.

chunks.text_en: The Direct Investment Promotion Authority announces its annual plan regarding tenders and qualifications to be issued, based on Article 33 of Law No. 49 of 2016 concerning Public Tenders, as follows: <table_caption>Investment Promotion Authority Project Summary</table_caption> <table> | Subject The company is prohibited from engaging in activities stipulated by Cabinet Resolution No. 75 of 2015 regarding the determination of a list of investments not subject to the provisions of Law No. 116 of 2013.

chunks.title: Article (8)
chunks.text_en: The capital owner is exclusively responsible for the following matters: 1. Amending the company's articles of association. 2. Dissolving and liquidating the company. 3. Merging, converting, or splitting the company. 4. Increasing or decreasing the company's capital. 5. Dismissing the company's manager, or restricting their authority. Subject to the provisions of merger, conversion, and split stipulated in Companies Law No. 1 of 2016, as amended by Law No. 15 of 2017, and its executive regulations, the aforementioned decisions shall be effective upon their registration in the Commercial Register without the need for formal notarization.

clauses.text_en: Amending the company's articles of association.
Dissolving and liquidating the company.
Merging, converting, or splitting the company.
Increasing or decreasing the company's capital.
Dismissing the company's manager, or restricting their authority.

chunks.title: Article (9)
chunks.text_en: The company's fiscal year begins in January and ends in December each year, with the exception of the first fiscal year, which starts from the date of the company's registration in the Commercial Register and ends on the specified date for the end of the following fiscal year.

chunks.title: Article (5)
chunks.text_en: The company's capital is set at 31,000 Kuwaiti Dinars ("only thirty-one thousand Kuwaiti Dinars "), with a total of 100 cash shares. The value of one cash share is 310 Kuwaiti Dinars ("only three hundred and ten Kuwaiti Dinars ") and is indivisible. Company Capital Shares Breakdown | No. | Shareholder Name | Number of Shares | Share Type | Value in Dinar | |:----|:-----------------|:-----------------|:-----------|:---------------| | 1 | Acture Middle East B.V. | 100 | Cash | 31000 | | Total | | | | 31000 |

chunks.title: Article (6)
chunks.text_en:
The company shall be managed by the capital owner, personally if a natural person, or by their representative if a legal entity. Company Owner/Manager Details | Mr. | Partner / Non-Partner | Nationality | ID Number | Address | |:----|:----------------------|:------------|:-------------|:---------------------| | Amr Haitham Hussein Bibi | Non-Partner | Kuwaiti | 280110301364 | Kuwait |
The company may appoint one or more managers to represent it before the judiciary and in dealings with third parties. The manager shall be responsible for its management before the owner, and the decision to appoint the manager shall not be effective until it is registered in the Commercial Register.

chunks.title: Article (7)
chunks.text_en: The manager represents the company in its relations with third parties and before the judiciary, and has the broadest authority to deal on its behalf and conclude all contracts and transactions falling within the company's objectives. Specifically, this includes appointing, suspending, and dismissing the company's agents and employees, determining their salaries, wages, and bonuses, receiving and paying amounts, signing and concluding all contracts, agreements, and deals related to the company's transactions or falling within its objectives. They also have the right to open company accounts or individual accounts (appointed by the capital owner) and the right to open bank accounts. The company manager shall exercise the diligence of a prudent person in exercising their powers and competencies. The manager shall be jointly liable towards the company or third parties for violating the provisions of the law and the provisions of these articles or for errors in management, in accordance with the rules stipulated in the chapter on joint-stock companies in Companies Law No. 1 of 2016, as amended by Law No. 15 of 2017, and its executive regulations. The company manager may not undertake the management of another competing company or a company with similar objectives, or contract with the company for their own account or for the account of a third party, unless with permission issued by

chunks.title: Article (10)
chunks.text_en: The company shall also be dissolved – and insofar as it does not conflict with the nature of a single-person company – for one of the reasons stipulated in Companies Law No. 1 of 2016, as amended by Law No. 15 of 2017, and its executive regulations. The dissolution of the company must be publicized, and the dissolution of the company shall not be invoked against third parties except from the date of publication. The company manager must follow up on the implementation of this procedure.

chunks.title: Article (11)
chunks.text_en: The liquidation of the company's assets upon its dissolution for any reason shall be carried out in accordance with the provisions contained in Companies Law No. 1 of 2016, as amended by Law No. 15 of 2017, and its executive regulations.

chunks.title: Article (12)
chunks.text_en: The owner shall be liable with their private assets for the company's obligations in the following cases: a) If they, in bad faith, dispose of it or cease its activity before the expiry of its term, or before achieving the purpose for which it was established. b) If they fail to separate their financial liability from the company's financial liability. c) If they conduct business on behalf of the company before it acquires legal personality.

chunks.title: Article (13)
chunks.text_en: All papers, contracts, publications, advertisements, correspondence, and other documents issued by the company must bear its trade name, a statement of its legal form, its registration number in the Commercial Register, a statement of the amount of capital and the paid-up portion thereof, and be followed by the phrase "Single Person Company (SPC) ".

chunks.title: Article (14)
chunks.text_en: The company – in matters not mentioned in these articles – shall be subject to the provisions of Companies Law No. 1 of 2016, as amended by Law No. 15 of 2017, and its executive regulations.

chunks.title: Article (15)
chunks.text_en: An original copy of this contract shall be kept at the company's main office and on its website. Based on Article (19) of Municipality Resolution No. (161/2009) regarding the classification, licensing, and registration of building and demolition contractors in Kuwait Municipality , which stipulates that a contractor may submit a request to the Municipality to cancel their registration before its expiry or to cancel the classification, and the committee stipulated in Article (1) of this regulation may issue its decision thereon after fulfilling the following two conditions: 1- The contractor has completed all contracted works. 2- Fifteen (15) days have elapsed from the date of publication of this request in the Official Gazette . The cancellation of the license or registration entails the refund of the bank guarantee submitted by the contractor, taking into account the last paragraph of Article (10) of this resolution. Accordingly, the Municipality appeals to anyone who has any claims against the aforementioned contractor regarding building contracting agreements concluded between them that have not yet been completed, or judicial attachments in their favor due to violating contracting works or dues arising from the contracting agreement, to review the Contractors Department at the Engineering Systems Department within a maximum period of fifteen days from this date. The Municipality will not consider any claims submitted after the prescribed period. Director General of the Municipality An original copy of this contract shall also be kept in the company's file with both the competent department in the Ministry of Commerce and Industry and the Direct Investment Promotion Authority . An original copy of this contract shall also be kept in the company's file with the Ministry of Justice . Any person wishing to obtain a certified true copy must request it from the company against a fee determined by the company management. - First Party: Acture Middle East B.V. - The foregoing was drawn up after this contract was read to those present and its occurrence confirmed. Signatory / Mishari Abdullah Al-Mutairi

clauses.text_en: The contractor has completed all contracted works.
Fifteen (15) days have elapsed from the date of publication of this request in the Official Gazette .

Kuwait University announces the offering of the following divisible practices: Kuwait University Divisible Practices Tender | No. | Practice No. | Subject | |:--------|:---|:---| | 1 | 2026-2025/1 | External facade cleaning works for Sabah Al-Salem University City buildings |
Practice documents can be obtained starting from Sunday, 20/04/2025, and on subsequent days during official working hours from the Procurement Department's website. (https://pur.ku.edu.kw) The deadline for submitting bids is Tuesday, 20/05/2025, at 12:00 PM. Please note that the preliminary meeting will be held on Thursday, 01/05/2025, at 10:30 AM, in the University Administration Building – at Sabah Al-Salem University City (Al-Shaddadiya ) – Services Department – 5th Floor – Meeting Room – Kuwait University . Bids received after this deadline will not be considered, and bids will be opened in a public session. - Instructions for Practitioners: - Bids must be submitted via CDs (3 copies), ensuring that the copying process for all files has been correctly completed before depositing the CDs in the submission envelope. They must be identical, similar, protected, and non-modifiable, and each must contain all practice documents signed and stamped with the practitioner's (company's) seal. - Inquiries are accepted within 10 days from the date of the announcement, and no inquiries will be accepted after the specified deadline. - The following paper documents must be submitted: - The original copy of the bid form and undertaking. - Initial insurance (as per the tender announcement). - Purchase receipt from the website. - A valid and effective copy of the national labor percentage certificate. - A valid "To Whom It May Concern" certificate issued by the Public Authority for Manpower stating that there is no suspension on the practitioner's file. - The envelope designated for the practice. - The bid will be excluded if any document mentioned in the instructions is not submitted. - Bids shall be placed in the designated box at the Procurement Department – in Sabah Al-Salem Suppliers City . Note: Withdrawal of tenders for specialized companies in this field, provided they are registered with the University's Supplier Affairs Department and registered with the Central Agency for Public Tenders , during official working hours. The deadline for submitting bids is 1:00 PM on Monday, 2025/05/12. Bids shall remain valid and cannot be withdrawn for a period of (90) ninety days from the date of opening the bid envelopes. The bidder must also deposit with their bid a preliminary bid bond of (1,000 K.D.) one thousand Kuwaiti Dinars only, and this bond must be valid and effective for the duration of the bid's validity." Acting Director General

chunks.title: Announcement
chunks.text_en: Kuwait Credit Bank announces the re-issuance of Practice No. (40-2025/2024) concerning the maintenance and upgrade of the electronic door system for all branches of Kuwait Credit Bank . Interested parties wishing to submit their bids should visit the Bank to purchase the booklet of terms and technical specifications for the aforementioned practice, according to the details specified below:

chunks.title: Submitted Bid Conditions
chunks.text_en:
The bidder must submit a technical offer and a financial offer in two sealed envelopes, one for the technical offer and the other for the financial offer, ensuring that a copy of the bid is attached on a flash drive, in accordance with the following conditions: - The technical envelope must contain the following: - Initial security deposit in the form of an unconditional, unreserved, and irrevocable letter of guarantee for the amount of (K.D. 2,000 ) "only two thousand Kuwaiti Dinars", issued by one of the banks accredited by the State of Kuwait in the name and for the benefit of Kuwait Credit Bank . This security deposit must be valid for at least three months from the last date for receiving bids. Any bid not accompanied by the full amount of this security deposit will be excluded. Initial security deposits may not be refunded until (90) ninety days have passed from the closing date of the practice or when the winning bidder submits the final security deposit and signs the contract, unless the practice is cancelled. Kuwait Credit Bank will not pay any interest on the amount of this security deposit, and the bidder's creditors may not seize its amount. - General and special conditions and technical data for the submitted offer. - Complete data on Kuwaiti subcontractors or individuals who may be assigned part of the execution if required by the practice documents. - Any other documents or data that may be required by the practice documents. - The financial envelope must contain the following: - Bid format approved by the bidder. - Price lists and bills of quantities. - Any other elements that may affect the financial value of the offer as required by the tender conditions. - Any other documents or data required by the practice documents. chunks.title: Announcement
chunks.text_en:
The Internal Purchasing Committee at Kuwait National Petroleum Company – one of the subsidiaries of Kuwait Petroleum Corporation – announces the issuance of Practice No.: RFQ/1055879 – MAA-D/C-Gate Valves, in accordance with the general and special terms and specifications contained in the practice documents. These documents can be obtained by visiting the unified electronic commercial platform of Kuwait Petroleum Corporation and its subsidiaries: ((https://ktendering.com.kw)) Tender Schedule for RFQ/1055879 | Issuance | Preliminary Meeting | Closing | Initial Security | |:---|:---|:---|:---| | 20/4/2025 | Not Applicable | 15/5/2025 | 1,000 KD valid for 90 days |
The final deadline for submitting bids is Thursday, 15/5/2025, at 1:00 PM local time of the State of Kuwait (13:00 Noon), noting that the bid must be submitted through the electronic commercial platform mentioned above. The practitioner must submit the initial security in favor of Kuwait National Petroleum Company in an envelope bearing the name and address of the practice and the closing date, and place it in the designated box located on the first floor, reception hall, at the Kuwait National Petroleum Company Main Office building in Al-Ahmadi , no later than 1:00 PM (13:00 Noon) on Thursday, 15/5/2025, with a copy of the initial security attached to the bid. It is noted that this practice is general and indivisible, such that the supply shall be from the unified qualification list of the Central Qualification Committee mentioned in the practice documents. No single person or group of persons combined may register under more than one name during classification, or submit more than one bid in a tender, except for alternative bids where the tender conditions permit their submission. However, a person who has a share in separate companies, each independent with its legal personality, finances, engineers, employees, and general administrative apparatus, may, during classification, register himself and each of his independent companies. The bid of a tenderer who violates this article shall be deemed null and void by operation of law, in accordance with Article No. (29) of Law No. (49) of 2016 regarding Public Tenders . **Note:** All required documents and certificates must be attached to the bid through the electronic commercial platform as specified in the practice documents. The Internal Purchasing Committee wishes to draw the attention of gentlemen/practitioners who have inquiries or reservations regarding the technical specifications to submit such requests at most (two weeks) before the closing date (for this practice). These requests will be rejected and will not be considered for any reason whatsoever after this deadline.
